title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 1
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:

"Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither tall nor was he short (like a dwarf... He was of medium stature). In complexion, he was neither very white like lime, nor very dark, nor brown which results in darkness (he was illuminant, more luminous than even the full-moon on the 14th night).The hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither very straight nor very curly (but slightly wavy). When he attained the age of forty, Allah the Almighty granted him nubuwwah (prophethood).He lived for ten years in Makkah (commentary) and in Madina for ten years. At that time there were not more than twenty white hair on his mubarak (blessed) head and beard." (This will be described in detail in the chapter on white hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither tall nor was he short (like a dwarf... He was of medium stature). In complexion, he was neither very white like lime, nor very dark, nor brown which results in darkness (he was illuminant, more luminous than even the full-moon on the 14th night).The hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither very straight nor very curly (but slightly wavy). When he attained the age of forty, Allah the Almighty granted him nubuwwah (prophethood).He lived for ten years in Makkah (commentary) and in Madina for ten years. At that time there were not more than twenty white hair on his mubarak (blessed) head and beard." (This will be described in detail in the chapter on white hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو رَجَاءٍ قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ رَبِيعَةَ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَهُ، يَقُولُ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، لَيْسَ بِالطَّوِيلِ الْبَائِنِ، وَلاَ بِالْقَصِيرِ، وَلاَ بِالأَبْيَضِ الأَمْهَقِ، وَلاَ بِالآدَمِ، وَلاَ بِالْجَعْدِ الْقَطَطِ، وَلاَ بِالسَّبْطِ، بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى عَلَى رَأْسِ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً، فَأَقَامَ بِمَكَّةَ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ، وَبِالْمَدِينَةِ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ، وَتَوَفَّاهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى عَلَى رَأْسِ سِتِّينَ سَنَةً، وَلَيْسَ فِي رَأْسِهِ وَلِحْيَتِهِ عِشْرُونَ شَعَرَةً بَيْضَاءَ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 1

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 2
englishRaw: Anas bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was of a medium stature, he was neither very tall nor very short. He was very handsome, of medium built and his hair was neither very curly nor very straight (but was slightly wavy). He had a wheat-coloured complexion. When he walked, he leaned forward slightly".
body: Anas bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was of a medium stature, he was neither very tall nor very short. He was very handsome, of medium built and his hair was neither very curly nor very straight (but was slightly wavy). He had a wheat-coloured complexion. When he walked, he leaned forward slightly".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ الْبَصْرِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ رَبْعَةً، لَيْسَ بِالطَّوِيلِ وَلا بِالْقَصِيرِ، حَسَنَ الْجِسْمِ، وَكَانَ شَعَرُهُ لَيْسَ بِجَعْدٍ، وَلا سَبْطٍ أَسْمَرَ اللَّوْنِ، إِذَا مَشَى يَتَكَفَّأُ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 2

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 3
englishRaw:
Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him) relates that:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was a man of a medium build, (slightly tall, as explained before); he had broad shoulders (from which we may gather that he had a wide chest); he had dense hair which reached his ear-lobes; he wore a red striped lungi (a cloth worn around the legs) and shawl. I never saw anybody or anything more handsome than him".
narrator: Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him) relates that:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was a man of a medium build, (slightly tall, as explained before); he had broad shoulders (from which we may gather that he had a wide chest); he had dense hair which reached his ear-lobes; he wore a red striped lungi (a cloth worn around the legs) and shawl. I never saw anybody or anything more handsome than him".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ الْبَرَاءَ بْنَ عَازِبٍ، يَقُولُ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، رَجُلا مَرْبُوعًا، بَعِيدَ مَا بَيْنَ الْمَنْكِبَيْنِ، عَظِيمَ الْجُمَّةِ إِلَى شَحْمَةِ أُذُنَيْهِ الْيُسْرَى، عَلَيْهِ حُلَّةٌ حَمْرَاءُ، مَا رَأَيْتُ شَيْئًا قَطُّ أَحْسَنَ مِنْهُ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 3

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 4
englishRaw:
Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I never seen someone with long hair and red clothing more handsome than Messenger of Allah ﷺ. His hair reached his shoulders. The portion between his two shoulders was wide. He was neither very tall nor very short".:
narrator: Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I never seen someone with long hair and red clothing more handsome than Messenger of Allah ﷺ. His hair reached his shoulders. The portion between his two shoulders was wide. He was neither very tall nor very short".:
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ عَازِبٍ، قَالَ: مَا رَأَيْتُ مِنْ ذِي لِمَّةٍ فِي حُلَّةٍ حَمْرَاءَ أَحْسَنَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، لَهُ شَعَرٌ يَضْرِبُ مَنْكِبَيْهِ، بَعِيدُ مَا بَيْنَ الْمَنْكِبَيْنِ، لَمْ يَكُنْ بِالْقَصِيرِ، وَلا بِالطَّوِيلِ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 4

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 5
englishRaw:
It is reported from Ali (may Allah be pleased with him):
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither very tall nor very short. The soles of both feet were very fleshed. (This quality is praiseworthy in a man as it denotes strength and courage but is not praiseworthy for a woman). He had a large head. The joints of the bones was also large. There was a thin line of hair from the chest to the navel. When Messenger of Allah ﷺ walked, it appeared that he was descending from a high place". Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) says: "I did not see anyone like him neither before him, nor after him".
narrator: It is reported from Ali (may Allah be pleased with him):
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither very tall nor very short. The soles of both feet were very fleshed. (This quality is praiseworthy in a man as it denotes strength and courage but is not praiseworthy for a woman). He had a large head. The joints of the bones was also large. There was a thin line of hair from the chest to the navel. When Messenger of Allah ﷺ walked, it appeared that he was descending from a high place". Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) says: "I did not see anyone like him neither before him, nor after him".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْمَسْعُودِيُّ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ هُرْمُزَ، عَنْ نَافِعِ بْنِ جُبَيْرِ بْنِ مُطْعِمٍ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، قَالَ: لَمْ يَكُنِ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ بِالطَّوِيلِ، وَلا بِالْقَصِيرِ، شَثْنُ الْكَفَّيْنِ وَالْقَدَمَيْنِ، ضَخْمُ الرَّأْسِ، ضَخْمُ الْكَرَادِيسِ، طَوِيلُ الْمَسْرُبَةِ، إِذَا مَشَى تَكَفَّأَ تَكَفُّؤًا، كَأَنَّمَا يَنْحَطُّ مِنْ صَبَبٍ، لَمْ أَرَ قَبْلَهُ، وَلا بَعْدَهُ مِثْلَهُ، ﷺ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 5

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 6
englishRaw:
It is related from Ebrahim bin Muhammad (may Allah be pleased with him) who is from the sons (grand sons of Ali may Allah be pleased with him, that whenever Ali(may Allah be pleased with him)described the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he used to say:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither very tall nor short, but of a medium stature among people. His hair was neither very curly nor very straight, but had a slight wave in it. He did not have a big body nor a round face, but his mubaarak face was slightly round (meaning he did not have a fully round face nor a fully elongated face, bur in between the two). The complexion of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was white with redness in it. The mubaarak eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were extremely black. His eyelashes were long. The joints of the body (e.g. elbows and knees etc.) were large, likewise the portion between the two shoulders was broad and fully fleshed. There was no hair (more than normal) on his body. (Some people have profuse hair on their body. Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not have hair on the parts of his body, besides places like the arms and legs etc.) He had a thin line of hair running from the chest to the navel. The hands and feet of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were fully fleshed. When he walked, he lifted his legs with vigor, as if he were descending to a low-lying place. When he addressed a person he turned his whole body towards that person. (He did not only turn his face towards the person he addressed, as this is considered impolite, and sometimes, it even denotes pride. Messenger of Allah ﷺ faced the person he spoke to, with his chest and body. Some scholars have translated this as, when Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressed someone, he completely turned his face towards that person, and did not give a side glance. This is not a suitable translation). The seal of prophethood was situated between his shoulders. He was a last of all prophets. He was the most generous and the most truthful. He was the most kind-hearted and came from a most noble family. (It means his character, family back-ground and everything else was of the best). Any person who saw him suddenly would become awe-inspired. Messenger of Allah ﷺ had such a great personality and dignity, that the person who saw him for the first time, because of his awe-inspiring personality, would be overcome with a feeling of profound respect. Firstly, there is a ro`b (awe) for physical beauty, with this when other Kamaalat are added what more could then be said of the ro'b (awe). Besides, the special attributes and qualities granted to Messenger of Allah ﷺ ro'b (awe) is also one of the special qualities granted to him). Anyone who came in close contact with him, and knew his excellent character was smitten with the love of his excellent attributes. Anyone who described his noble features can only say: "I have not seen anyone like Messenger of Allah ﷺ neither before nor after him."
narrator: It is related from Ebrahim bin Muhammad (may Allah be pleased with him) who is from the sons (grand sons of Ali may Allah be pleased with him, that whenever Ali(may Allah be pleased with him)described the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he used to say:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was neither very tall nor short, but of a medium stature among people. His hair was neither very curly nor very straight, but had a slight wave in it. He did not have a big body nor a round face, but his mubaarak face was slightly round (meaning he did not have a fully round face nor a fully elongated face, bur in between the two). The complexion of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was white with redness in it. The mubaarak eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were extremely black. His eyelashes were long. The joints of the body (e.g. elbows and knees etc.) were large, likewise the portion between the two shoulders was broad and fully fleshed. There was no hair (more than normal) on his body. (Some people have profuse hair on their body. Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not have hair on the parts of his body, besides places like the arms and legs etc.) He had a thin line of hair running from the chest to the navel. The hands and feet of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were fully fleshed. When he walked, he lifted his legs with vigor, as if he were descending to a low-lying place. When he addressed a person he turned his whole body towards that person. (He did not only turn his face towards the person he addressed, as this is considered impolite, and sometimes, it even denotes pride. Messenger of Allah ﷺ faced the person he spoke to, with his chest and body. Some scholars have translated this as, when Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressed someone, he completely turned his face towards that person, and did not give a side glance. This is not a suitable translation). The seal of prophethood was situated between his shoulders. He was a last of all prophets. He was the most generous and the most truthful. He was the most kind-hearted and came from a most noble family. (It means his character, family back-ground and everything else was of the best). Any person who saw him suddenly would become awe-inspired. Messenger of Allah ﷺ had such a great personality and dignity, that the person who saw him for the first time, because of his awe-inspiring personality, would be overcome with a feeling of profound respect. Firstly, there is a ro`b (awe) for physical beauty, with this when other Kamaalat are added what more could then be said of the ro'b (awe). Besides, the special attributes and qualities granted to Messenger of Allah ﷺ ro'b (awe) is also one of the special qualities granted to him). Anyone who came in close contact with him, and knew his excellent character was smitten with the love of his excellent attributes. Anyone who described his noble features can only say: "I have not seen anyone like Messenger of Allah ﷺ neither before nor after him."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدَةَ الضَّبِّيُّ الْبَصْرِيُّ، وَعَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، وَأَبُو جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ وَهُوَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَلِيمَةَ، وَالْمَعْنَى وَاحِدٌ، قَالُوا: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللهِ مَوْلَى غُفْرَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَلِيٌّ إِذَا وَصَفَ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، قَالَ: لَمْ يَكُنْ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ بِالطَّوِيلِ الْمُمَّغِطِ، وَلا بِالْقَصِيرِ الْمُتَرَدِّدِ، وَكَانَ رَبْعَةً مِنَ الْقَوْمِ، لَمْ يَكُنْ بِالْجَعْدِ الْقَطَطِ، وَلا بِالسَّبْطِ، كَانَ جَعْدًا رَجِلا، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ بِالْمُطَهَّمِ، وَلا بِالْمُكَلْثَمِ، وَكَانَ فِي وَجْهِهِ تَدْوِيرٌ، أَبْيَضُ مُشَرَبٌ، أَدْعَجُ الْعَيْنَيْنِ، أَهْدَبُ الأَشْفَارِ، جَلِيلُ الْمُشَاشِ وَالْكَتَدِ، أَجْرَدُ، ذُو مَسْرُبَةٍ، شَثْنُ الْكَفَّيْنِ وَالْقَدَمَيْنِ، إِذَا مَشَى كَأَنَّمَا يَنْحَطُّ فِي صَبَبٍ، وَإِذَا الْتَفَتَ الْتَفَتَ مَعًا، بَيْنَ كَتِفَيْهِ خَاتَمُ النُّبُوَّةِ، وَهُوَ خَاتَمُ النَّبِيِّينَ، أَجْوَدُ النَّاسِ صَدْرًا، وَأَصْدَقُ النَّاسِ لَهْجَةً، وَأَلْيَنُهُمْ عَرِيكَةً، وَأَكْرَمُهُمْ عِشْرَةً، مَنْ رَآهُ بَدِيهَةً هَابَهُ، وَمَنْ خَالَطَهُ مَعْرِفَةً أَحَبَّهُ، يَقُولُ نَاعِتُهُ: لَمْ أَرَ قَبْلَهُ، وَلا بَعْدَهُ مِثْلَهُ ﷺ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 6

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 7
englishRaw:
Hasan bin Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) reported:
                      "I inquired from my maternal uncle (Sayyiditina Fatimah may Allah be pleased with her's step brother) Hind bin Abi Haalah (may Allah be pleased with him) about the noble features of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He had often described the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in detail. I felt that I should hear from him personally, some of the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, so that I could make his description a proof and testimony for myself and also memorize them, and, if possible, try to emulate and adopt them. (The age of Sayyidna Hasan (may Allah be pleased with him) at the time of Messenger of Allah ﷺ's death was seven years. In view of his age he did not have the opportunity to realize fully the features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The uncle described the noble features by saying: "He had great qualities and attributes in him, others also held him in high esteem. His mubarak face shone like the full moon. He was slightly taller than a man of middle height, but shorter than a tall person. His mubarak head was moderately large. His mubarak hair was slightly twisted. If his hair became parted naturally in the middle he left it so, otherwise he did not habitually make an effort to part his hair in the middle. (This is a more respected translation).A question may arise that Messenger of Allah ﷺ habitually parted his hair as stated in Ahadith. The Ulama say that this was in the early periods where Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not make an effort to do so. According to this humble servant, the answer to this is bit difficult, because it was the principal of Messenger of Allah ﷺ to oppose the ways of the non-believers, and agree to the ways of Ahlul Kitab, he did not part his hair in the middle. For this reason, according to some Ulama, the best translation will be that he only parted his hair in the middle if it could be easily done, and when this could not be done easily, and a comb etc. was needed to do it, then he did not part his hair in the middle. Occasionally he used to part his hair in the middle with a comb etc. When the hair of Messenger of Allah (Sallallalhu alaihe wasallallam) was abundant, it used to pass over his ear-lobes). Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a very luminous complexion (colour), and a wide forehead. He had dense and fine hair on his eye brows. Both eye brows were separate and did not meet each other in the middle. There was a vein between them that used to expand when he became angry. His nose was prominent and had a nur and lustre on it. When one first looked at him, it seemed as if he had a large nose, but looking at it carefully showed that the lustre and beauty made it look large, otherwise in itself the nose was not large."His beard was full and dense. The pupil of his eye was black. His cheeks were full and full of flesh. The mouth of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was moderately wide. (He did not have a small mouth). His teeth were thin and bright. The front teeth had a slight space between them. There was a thin line of hair from the chest to the navel. His neck was beautiful and thin, like the neck of a statue shaved clean, the colour of which was clear, shining and beautiful like silver. All the parts of his body were of moderate size, and fully fleshed. His body was proportionately jointed. His chest and stomach were in line, but his chest was broad and wide. The space between his shoulders was wide. The bones of his joints were strong and large (denoting strength). When he removed his clothing, his body looked bright and had a lustre (or rather those parts of the body that were not covered by his clothing were also bright and shining, compared with those parts of the body that were covered by his clothing. According to this humble servant the latter translation is more appropriate). Between the chest and navel there was a thin line of hair. Besides this line neither the chest nor the stomach had other hair on it. Both sides, the shoulders and the upper portion of the chest had hair. His forearm was long and palms were wide. The palms and both feet were fully fleshed. The fingers and toes were moderately long. The soles of his feet were a bit deep. His feet were smooth, because of their cleanliness and smoothness the water did not remain there but flowed away quickly. When he walked, he lifted his legs with vigor, leaned slightly forward and placed his feet softly on the ground. He walked at a quick pace and took rather a long step. He did not take small steps. When he walked it seemed as if he was descending to a lower place. When he looked at something he turned his whole body towards it. He always looked down. His sight was focused more to the ground than towards the sky (A question may arise here that it is reported in Abu Daawud that Messenger of Allah ﷺ usually looked towards the sky. Both are reconciled thus: His habit was to look down towards the ground, but he also waited for the wahi (revelation), therefore while waiting he often looked towards the sky. Otherwise he usually looked down towards the ground.His modest habit was to look at something with a light eye, i.e. he looked at a thing, with modesty and bashfulness, hence he did not stare at anything. While walking he asked the Sahabah (may Allah be pleased with himm) to walk in front, and he himself walked behind. He made salaam to whomsoever he met"
narrator: Hasan bin Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) reported:
body: "I inquired from my maternal uncle (Sayyiditina Fatimah may Allah be pleased with her's step brother) Hind bin Abi Haalah (may Allah be pleased with him) about the noble features of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He had often described the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in detail. I felt that I should hear from him personally, some of the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, so that I could make his description a proof and testimony for myself and also memorize them, and, if possible, try to emulate and adopt them. (The age of Sayyidna Hasan (may Allah be pleased with him) at the time of Messenger of Allah ﷺ's death was seven years. In view of his age he did not have the opportunity to realize fully the features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The uncle described the noble features by saying: "He had great qualities and attributes in him, others also held him in high esteem. His mubarak face shone like the full moon. He was slightly taller than a man of middle height, but shorter than a tall person. His mubarak head was moderately large. His mubarak hair was slightly twisted. If his hair became parted naturally in the middle he left it so, otherwise he did not habitually make an effort to part his hair in the middle. (This is a more respected translation).A question may arise that Messenger of Allah ﷺ habitually parted his hair as stated in Ahadith. The Ulama say that this was in the early periods where Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not make an effort to do so. According to this humble servant, the answer to this is bit difficult, because it was the principal of Messenger of Allah ﷺ to oppose the ways of the non-believers, and agree to the ways of Ahlul Kitab, he did not part his hair in the middle. For this reason, according to some Ulama, the best translation will be that he only parted his hair in the middle if it could be easily done, and when this could not be done easily, and a comb etc. was needed to do it, then he did not part his hair in the middle. Occasionally he used to part his hair in the middle with a comb etc. When the hair of Messenger of Allah (Sallallalhu alaihe wasallallam) was abundant, it used to pass over his ear-lobes). Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a very luminous complexion (colour), and a wide forehead. He had dense and fine hair on his eye brows. Both eye brows were separate and did not meet each other in the middle. There was a vein between them that used to expand when he became angry. His nose was prominent and had a nur and lustre on it. When one first looked at him, it seemed as if he had a large nose, but looking at it carefully showed that the lustre and beauty made it look large, otherwise in itself the nose was not large."His beard was full and dense. The pupil of his eye was black. His cheeks were full and full of flesh. The mouth of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was moderately wide. (He did not have a small mouth). His teeth were thin and bright. The front teeth had a slight space between them. There was a thin line of hair from the chest to the navel. His neck was beautiful and thin, like the neck of a statue shaved clean, the colour of which was clear, shining and beautiful like silver. All the parts of his body were of moderate size, and fully fleshed. His body was proportionately jointed. His chest and stomach were in line, but his chest was broad and wide. The space between his shoulders was wide. The bones of his joints were strong and large (denoting strength). When he removed his clothing, his body looked bright and had a lustre (or rather those parts of the body that were not covered by his clothing were also bright and shining, compared with those parts of the body that were covered by his clothing. According to this humble servant the latter translation is more appropriate). Between the chest and navel there was a thin line of hair. Besides this line neither the chest nor the stomach had other hair on it. Both sides, the shoulders and the upper portion of the chest had hair. His forearm was long and palms were wide. The palms and both feet were fully fleshed. The fingers and toes were moderately long. The soles of his feet were a bit deep. His feet were smooth, because of their cleanliness and smoothness the water did not remain there but flowed away quickly. When he walked, he lifted his legs with vigor, leaned slightly forward and placed his feet softly on the ground. He walked at a quick pace and took rather a long step. He did not take small steps. When he walked it seemed as if he was descending to a lower place. When he looked at something he turned his whole body towards it. He always looked down. His sight was focused more to the ground than towards the sky (A question may arise here that it is reported in Abu Daawud that Messenger of Allah ﷺ usually looked towards the sky. Both are reconciled thus: His habit was to look down towards the ground, but he also waited for the wahi (revelation), therefore while waiting he often looked towards the sky. Otherwise he usually looked down towards the ground.His modest habit was to look at something with a light eye, i.e. he looked at a thing, with modesty and bashfulness, hence he did not stare at anything. While walking he asked the Sahabah (may Allah be pleased with himm) to walk in front, and he himself walked behind. He made salaam to whomsoever he met"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جُمَيْعُ بْنُ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْعِجْلِيُّ، إِمْلاءً عَلَيْنَا مِنْ كِتَابِهِ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي تَمِيمٍ، مِنْ وَلَدِ أَبِي هَالَةَ زَوْجِ خَدِيجَةَ، يُكَنَى أَبَا عَبْدِ اللهِ، عَنِ ابْنٍ لأَبِي هَالَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ خَالِي هِنْدَ بْنَ أَبِي هَالَةَ، وَكَانَ وَصَّافًا، عَنْ حِلْيَةِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، وَأَنَا أَشْتَهِي أَنْ يَصِفَ لِي مِنْهَا شَيْئًا أَتَعَلَّقُ بِهِ، فَقَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ فَخْمًا مُفَخَّمًا، يَتَلأْلأُ وَجْهُهُ، تَلأْلُؤَ الْقَمَرِ لَيْلَةَ الْبَدْرِ، أَطْوَلُ مِنَ الْمَرْبُوعِ، وَأَقْصَرُ مِنَ الْمُشَذَّبِ، عَظِيمُ الْهَامَةِ، رَجِلُ الشَّعْرِ، إِنِ انْفَرَقَتْ عَقِيقَتُهُ فَرَّقَهَا، وَإِلا فَلا يُجَاوِزُ شَعَرُهُ شَحْمَةَ أُذُنَيْهِ، إِذَا هُوَ وَفَّرَهُ، أَزْهَرُ اللَّوْنِ، وَاسِعُ الْجَبِينِ، أَزَجُّ الْحَوَاجِبِ، سَوَابِغَ فِي غَيْرِ قَرَنٍ، بَيْنَهُمَا عِرْقٌ، يُدِرُّهُ الْغَضَبُ، أَقْنَى الْعِرْنَيْنِ، لَهُ نُورٌ يَعْلُوهُ، يَحْسَبُهُ مَنْ لَمْ يَتَأَمَّلْهُ أَشَمَّ، كَثُّ اللِّحْيَةِ، سَهْلُ الْخدَّيْنِ، ضَلِيعُ الْفَمِ، مُفْلَجُ الأَسْنَانِ، دَقِيقُ الْمَسْرُبَةِ، كَأَنَّ عُنُقَهُ جِيدُ دُمْيَةٍ، فِي صَفَاءِ الْفِضَّةِ، مُعْتَدِلُ الْخَلْقِ، بَادِنٌ مُتَمَاسِكٌ، سَوَاءُ الْبَطْنِ وَالصَّدْرِ، عَرِيضُ الصَّدْرِ، بَعِيدُ مَا بَيْنَ الْمَنْكِبَيْنِ، ضَخْمُ الْكَرَادِيسِ، أَنْوَرُ الْمُتَجَرَّدِ، مَوْصُولُ مَا بَيْنَ اللَّبَّةِ وَالسُّرَّةِ بِشَعَرٍ يَجْرِي كَالْخَطِّ، عَارِي الثَّدْيَيْنِ وَالْبَطْنِ مِمَّا سِوَى ذَلِكَ، أَشْعَرُ الذِّرَاعَيْنِ، وَالْمَنْكِبَيْنِ، وَأَعَالِي الصَّدْرِ، طَوِيلُ الزَّنْدَيْنِ، رَحْبُ الرَّاحَةِ، شَثْنُ الْكَفَّيْنِ وَالْقَدَمَيْنِ، سَائِلُ الأَطْرَافِ أَوْ قَالَ: شَائِلُ الأَطْرَافِ خَمْصَانُ الأَخْمَصَيْنِ، مَسِيحُ الْقَدَمَيْنِ، يَنْبُو عَنْهُمَا الْمَاءُ، إِذَا زَالَ، زَالَ قَلِعًا، يَخْطُو تَكَفِّيًا، وَيَمْشِي هَوْنًا، ذَرِيعُ الْمِشْيَةِ، إِذَا مَشَى كَأَنَّمَا يَنْحَطُّ مِنْ صَبَبٍ، وَإِذَا الْتَفَتَ الْتَفَتَ جَمِيعًا، خَافِضُ الطَّرْفِ، نَظَرُهُ إِلَى الأَرْضِ، أَطْوَلُ مِنْ نَظَرِهِ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ، جُلُّ نَظَرِهِ الْمُلاحَظَةُ، يَسُوقُ أَصْحَابَهُ، وَيَبْدَأُ مَنْ لَقِيَ بِالسَّلامِ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 7

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 8
englishRaw:
Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a wide mouth. There were red lines in the whiteness of his eyes. He had little flesh on his heels."
narrator: Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a wide mouth. There were red lines in the whiteness of his eyes. He had little flesh on his heels."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُوسَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْبٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ جَابِرَ بْنَ سَمُرَةَ، يَقُولُ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ ضَلِيعَ الْفَمِ، أَشْكَلَ الْعَيْنِ، مَنْهُوسَ الْعَقِبِ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 8

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 9
englishRaw:
It is related from Jaabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said:
                      "I once saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ on the night of a full moon. On that night he wore red clothing. At times I looked at the full moon and at times at Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Ultimately I came to the conclusion that Messenger of Allah ﷺ was more handsome, beautiful and more radiant than the full moon."
narrator: It is related from Jaabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said:
body: "I once saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ on the night of a full moon. On that night he wore red clothing. At times I looked at the full moon and at times at Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Ultimately I came to the conclusion that Messenger of Allah ﷺ was more handsome, beautiful and more radiant than the full moon."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّادُ بْنُ السَّرِيِّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْثَرُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَوَّارٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ سَمُرَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، فِي لَيْلَةٍ إِضْحِيَانٍ، وَعَلَيْهِ حُلَّةٌ حَمْرَاءُ، فَجَعَلْتُ أَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِ وَإِلَى الْقَمَرِ، فَلَهُوَ عِنْدِي أَحْسَنُ مِنَ الْقَمَرِ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 9

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 10
englishRaw:
Abu-Ishaaq (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "A person once asked Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him), "Was the face of Messenger of Allah ﷺ shining like a sword?" He replied: "No but like a full-moon with its roundness.":
narrator: Abu-Ishaaq (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "A person once asked Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him), "Was the face of Messenger of Allah ﷺ shining like a sword?" He replied: "No but like a full-moon with its roundness.":
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيعٍ، حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرُّؤَاسِيُّ، عَنْ زُهَيْرٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ الْبَرَاءَ بْنَ عَازِبٍ: أَكَانَ وَجْهُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ مِثْلَ السَّيْفِ؟ قَالَ: لا، بَلْ مِثْلَ الْقَمَرِ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 10

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 11
englishRaw:
Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was so clean, clear, beautiful and handsome, as though his body was covered and molded in silver. His hair was slightly curled.":
narrator: Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was so clean, clear, beautiful and handsome, as though his body was covered and molded in silver. His hair was slightly curled.":
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الْمَصَاحِفِيُّ سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ سَلْمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا النَّضْرُ بْنُ شُمَيْلٍ، عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ أَبِي الأَخْضَرِ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ أَبْيَضَ كَأَنَّمَا صِيغَ مِنْ فِضَّةٍ، رَجِلَ الشَّعْرِ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 11

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 12
englishRaw:
Jaabir bin Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) narrates from Messenger of Allah ﷺ that he said:
                      "The Ambiyaa (Prophets) were shown to me. I saw Musa (Alaihis salaam), he had a thin body, like one from among the tribe of Shanu'ah. I saw Esa (Alaihis salaam). From among all those whom I have seen, he somewhat resembled Urwah bin Masud. I saw Ebrahim (Alaihis salaam). From among all those that I have seen, I, more or less, look like him. In the same manner I saw Jibra-eel (Alaihis salaam). From among all those I had seen, he more or less looked like Dihyah Kalbi."
narrator: Jaabir bin Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) narrates from Messenger of Allah ﷺ that he said:
body: "The Ambiyaa (Prophets) were shown to me. I saw Musa (Alaihis salaam), he had a thin body, like one from among the tribe of Shanu'ah. I saw Esa (Alaihis salaam). From among all those whom I have seen, he somewhat resembled Urwah bin Masud. I saw Ebrahim (Alaihis salaam). From among all those that I have seen, I, more or less, look like him. In the same manner I saw Jibra-eel (Alaihis salaam). From among all those I had seen, he more or less looked like Dihyah Kalbi."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي اللَّيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللهِ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، قَالَ: عُرِضَ عَلَيَّ الأَنْبِيَاءُ، فَإِذَا مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ، ضَرْبٌ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ، كَأَنَّهُ مِنْ رِجَالِ شَنُوءَةَ، وَرَأَيْتُ عِيسَى بْنَ مَرْيَمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ، فَإِذَا أَقْرَبُ مَنْ رَأَيْتُ بِهِ شَبَهًا عُرْوَةُ بْنُ مَسْعُودٍ، وَرَأَيْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ، فَإِذَا أَقْرَبُ مَنْ رَأَيْتُ بِهِ شَبَهًا صَاحِبُكُمْ، يَعْنِي نَفْسَهُ، وَرَأَيْتُ جِبْرِيلَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلامُ، فَإِذَا أَقْرَبُ مَنْ رَأَيْتُ بِهِ شَبَهًا دِحْيَةُ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 12

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 13
englishRaw:
Sa'eed Jariri (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I heard Abu Tufayl (may Allah be pleased with him) say: "There is no one left on the face of this Earth, besides me who had seen Messenger of Allah ﷺ." I asked him to describe to me the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a white complexion, which was slightly reddish, and had a medium sized body.":
narrator: Sa'eed Jariri (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I heard Abu Tufayl (may Allah be pleased with him) say: "There is no one left on the face of this Earth, besides me who had seen Messenger of Allah ﷺ." I asked him to describe to me the noble features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a white complexion, which was slightly reddish, and had a medium sized body.":
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيعٍ، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، الْمَعْنَى وَاحِدٌ، قَالا: أَخْبَرَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ الْجُرَيْرِيِّ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الطُّفَيْلِ، يَقُولُ: رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ وَمَا بَقِيَ عَلَى وَجْهِ الأَرْضِ أَحَدٌ رَآهُ غَيْرِي، قُلْتُ: صِفْهُ لِي، قَالَ: كَانَ أَبْيَضَ، مَلِيحًا، مُقَصَّدًا.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 13

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 14
englishRaw:
Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "The front teeth of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were a bit wide (spread out). They were spaced out and not close together. When Messenger of Allah ﷺ talked, an illumination emitting from his teeth could be seen."
narrator: Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "The front teeth of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were a bit wide (spread out). They were spaced out and not close together. When Messenger of Allah ﷺ talked, an illumination emitting from his teeth could be seen."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ الْمُنْذِرِ الْحِزَامِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ الزُّهْرِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ابْنُ أَخِي مُوسَى بْنِ عُقْبَةَ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُقْبَةَ، عَنْ كُرَيْبٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ أَفْلَجَ الثَّنِيَّتَيْنِ، إِذَا تَكَلَّمَ رُئِيَ كَالنُّورِ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ بَيْنِ ثَنَايَاهُ.
references: Book of The Noble Features Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 14

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 15
englishRaw:
Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him) said:
                      "My (maternal) aunt took to me to Prophet ﷺ and said to him, this nephew of mine is ill. Messenger of Allah ﷺ wiped his holy hand over my head and made for barakah for me. (According to some Ulama, Messenger of Allah ﷺ wiped his hand over his head, meant that he Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him) had a pain in the head. The opinion of this weak and humble servant is that it is better if this is taken to mean that Prophet ﷺ wiped his hands in kindness on the head of Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him). Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him) was born in the second year Hijri, and at the time of Prophet ﷺ's death, his age was not more than 8 or 9 years. That is why Prophet ﷺ wiped his Holy hand in kindness, as is the customs of the great personalities. Prophet ﷺ also gave him the water of wudu to drink as a cure, as will be stated further, or he may have prescribed another remedy, especially when we learn also in a narration in Bukhari thay he Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him)was suffering from pain in his leg). When Prophet ﷺ performed Wudu, I drank the water of that wudu. (Prophet ﷺ may have performed wudu for some reason, but here it is clear that Prophet ﷺ performed wudu so that the water could be used as a remedy and medicine). I saw the seal of Prophethood, which was like the knot on a mosquito net or bedstead." (Which is the size of a pigeon's egg in roundness). The Ulama differ in the translation of this word. Some have translated it in another manner. Imam Nawawi, the famous commentator of Sahih Muslim, preferred the translation I have chosen).
narrator: Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him) said:
body: "My (maternal) aunt took to me to Prophet ﷺ and said to him, this nephew of mine is ill. Messenger of Allah ﷺ wiped his holy hand over my head and made for barakah for me. (According to some Ulama, Messenger of Allah ﷺ wiped his hand over his head, meant that he Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him) had a pain in the head. The opinion of this weak and humble servant is that it is better if this is taken to mean that Prophet ﷺ wiped his hands in kindness on the head of Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him). Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him) was born in the second year Hijri, and at the time of Prophet ﷺ's death, his age was not more than 8 or 9 years. That is why Prophet ﷺ wiped his Holy hand in kindness, as is the customs of the great personalities. Prophet ﷺ also gave him the water of wudu to drink as a cure, as will be stated further, or he may have prescribed another remedy, especially when we learn also in a narration in Bukhari thay he Sayyidina Saa-ib bin Yazid (may Allah be pleased with him)was suffering from pain in his leg). When Prophet ﷺ performed Wudu, I drank the water of that wudu. (Prophet ﷺ may have performed wudu for some reason, but here it is clear that Prophet ﷺ performed wudu so that the water could be used as a remedy and medicine). I saw the seal of Prophethood, which was like the knot on a mosquito net or bedstead." (Which is the size of a pigeon's egg in roundness). The Ulama differ in the translation of this word. Some have translated it in another manner. Imam Nawawi, the famous commentator of Sahih Muslim, preferred the translation I have chosen).
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو رَجَاءٍ قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنِ الْجَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ السَّائِبَ بْنَ يَزِيدَ، يَقُولُ: ذَهَبَتْ بِي خَالَتِي إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَقَالَتْ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، إِنَّ ابْنَ أُخْتِي وَجِعٌ فَمَسَحَ رَأْسِي وَدَعَا لِي بِالْبَرَكَةِ، وَتَوَضَّأَ، فَشَرِبْتُ مِنْ وَضُوئِهِ، وَقُمْتُ خَلْفَ ظَهْرِهِ، فَنَظَرْتُ إِلَى الْخَاتَمِ بَيْنَ كَتِفَيْهِ، فَإِذَا هُوَ مِثْلُ زِرِّ الْحَجَلَةِ.
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 15

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 16
englishRaw:
Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that:
"I saw the Seal of Prophethood of Messenger of Allah ﷺ between his two shoulders, which was like a red tumor (protruding flesh), the size of which was like that of a pigeon's egg"
narrator: Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that:
body: "I saw the Seal of Prophethood of Messenger of Allah ﷺ between his two shoulders, which was like a red tumor (protruding flesh), the size of which was like that of a pigeon's egg"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ الطَّالْقَانِيُّ، قَالَ: حدَّثَنَا أَيُّوبُ بْنُ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ سَمُرَةَ، قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ الْخَاتَمَ بَيْنَ كَتِفَيْ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، غُدَّةً حَمْرَاءَ، مِثْلَ بَيْضَةِ الْحَمَامَةِ.
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 16

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 17
englishRaw:
Rumaythah (may Allah be pleased with her) said:
                      "I heard this subject from Messenger of Allah ﷺ and at that time I was so near him, that if I wanted to, I could have kissed the Seal of Prophethood. That subject is: `Messenger of Allah ﷺ was talking about Sa's bin Mu'aadh (may Allah be pleased with him), that because of Sa'd's death, the `Arsh (Throne) of Allah shook'".
narrator: Rumaythah (may Allah be pleased with her) said:
body: "I heard this subject from Messenger of Allah ﷺ and at that time I was so near him, that if I wanted to, I could have kissed the Seal of Prophethood. That subject is: `Messenger of Allah ﷺ was talking about Sa's bin Mu'aadh (may Allah be pleased with him), that because of Sa'd's death, the `Arsh (Throne) of Allah shook'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُصْعَبٍ الْمَديَنِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يُوسُفُ بْنُ الْمَاجِشُونِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ جَدَّتِهِ رُمَيْثَةَ، قَالَتْ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، وَلَوْ أَشَاءُ أَنْ أُقَبِّلَ الْخَاتَمَ الَّذِي بَيْنَ كَتِفَيْهِ مِنْ قُرْبِهِ لَفَعَلْتُ، يَقُولُ لِسَعْدِ بْنِ مُعَاذٍ يَوْمَ مَاتَ: اهْتَزَّ لَهُ عَرْشُ الرَّحْمَنِ.
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 17

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 18
englishRaw:
Ebrahim bin Muhammad (may Allah be pleased with him), who is the grandson of Ali (may Allah be pleased with him said:
                      "Whenever Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) used to describe the noble attributes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he used to mentioned the complete hadith. He also used to say that the Seal of Prophethood was between his shoulders, and Messenger of Allah ﷺ was the seal of all prophets".
narrator: Ebrahim bin Muhammad (may Allah be pleased with him), who is the grandson of Ali (may Allah be pleased with him said:
body: "Whenever Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) used to describe the noble attributes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he used to mentioned the complete hadith. He also used to say that the Seal of Prophethood was between his shoulders, and Messenger of Allah ﷺ was the seal of all prophets".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدَةَ الضَّبِّيُّ، وَعَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، قَالُوا: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللهِ مَوْلَى غُفْرَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَلِيٌّ، إِذَا وَصَفَ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ فَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ بِطُولِهِ، وَقَالَ: بَيْنَ كَتِفَيْهِ خَاتَمُ النُّبُوَّةِ، وَهُوَ خَاتَمُ النَّبِيِّينَ.
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 18

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 19
englishRaw:
`Ilbaa bin Ahmar Al-yashkari says that the Sahaabi, Abu Zayd `Amr bin Akhtab Al-Ansaari may Allah be pleased with him said to me:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once asked me to massage his waist. When I began massaging the back, accidentally (by chance) my fingers touched the Seal of Prophethood. `Ilbaa (may Allah be pleased with him) says: `I asked Amr (may Allah be pleased with him), what is the Seal of Prophethood?' He replied: `It was a collection of few hair'".
narrator: `Ilbaa bin Ahmar Al-yashkari says that the Sahaabi, Abu Zayd `Amr bin Akhtab Al-Ansaari may Allah be pleased with him said to me:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once asked me to massage his waist. When I began massaging the back, accidentally (by chance) my fingers touched the Seal of Prophethood. `Ilbaa (may Allah be pleased with him) says: `I asked Amr (may Allah be pleased with him), what is the Seal of Prophethood?' He replied: `It was a collection of few hair'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَاصِمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَزْرَةُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عِلْبَاءُ بْنُ أَحْمَرَ الْيَشْكُرِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو زَيْدٍ عَمْرُو بْنُ أَخْطَبَ الأَنْصَارِيُّ، قَالَ: قَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ: يَا أَبَا زَيْدٍ، ادْنُ مِنِّي فَامْسَحْ ظَهْرِي، فَمَسَحْتُ ظَهْرَهُ، فَوَقَعَتْ أَصَابِعِي عَلَى الْخَاتَمِ قُلْتُ: وَمَا الْخَاتَمُ؟ قَالَ: شَعَرَاتٌ مُجْتَمِعَاتٌ.
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 19

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 20
englishRaw:
Buraydah bin (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "when Rasulalullah ﷺ came to Medinah, Salmaan Faarisi (may Allah be pleased with him) brought a tray which had fresh dates on it, and presented it to Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who asked:"O Salmaan, what dates are these?"He replied:"This is sadaqah for you and your companions"Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied:"We do not eat Sadaqah. Remove it from me."(The 'ulama differ in their opinions as to the meaning of the word "we". Some say it is Messenger of Allah ﷺ himself, and the plural is used as a mark of respect. Others explain that it is the ambiyaa (prophets). According to some it is Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his relatives, for whom it is not permissible to accept zakaah. According to this humble servant the third ihtimaal (supposition) is superior and more acceptable. Allaamah Munaawi's criticism of the third explanation is not forceful and weighty). On the next day this happened again. Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) brought a tray of fresh dates, and in reply to the question of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he replied: "O messenger of Allah, it is a present for you".Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to the Sahaabah (may Allah be pleased with him)m "Help yourselves". (Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ himself ate from it. Bayjuri explains this thus: Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) bringing the dates on both days in this manner was to investigate, and to make Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ his master. Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) was an 'Aalim (learned) of the old days. He lived for a hundred and fifty years and according to some, he lived three hundred years. He had seen the signs of Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the kitaabs of previous prophets, that he will not accept sadaqah, but shall accept presents and gifts, and the seal of Prophethood will be between his two shoulders after witnessing the first two signs).He then saw the seal of Prophethood on the back of Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ and embraced Islam. (At that time Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) was a slave of a Jew from the tribe of Banu Qurayzah.Messenger of Allah ﷺ purchased him(this is figuratively speaking. The fact is that Messenger of Allah ﷺ made him a Mukaatab - One who buys One's freedom for any agreed sum.) and paid Dirhams for him to become a Mukaatab, and also agreed that he(Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) should plant for the Jew date palms,(the amount of three hundred palms) and until these bore fruit to tend them. Messenger of Allah ﷺ planted the palms with his mubaarak hands and it was his mu'jizah(miracle) that all the palms bore fruit in the same year. One tree among these did not bear fruit. Upon investigating it was found that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) had planted this tree, and that it was not planted by Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ removed this palm and replanted it. Another mu'jizah Messenger of Allah ﷺ is that he planted the palms out of season and they bore fruit the same year.
narrator: Buraydah bin (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "when Rasulalullah ﷺ came to Medinah, Salmaan Faarisi (may Allah be pleased with him) brought a tray which had fresh dates on it, and presented it to Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who asked:"O Salmaan, what dates are these?"He replied:"This is sadaqah for you and your companions"Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied:"We do not eat Sadaqah. Remove it from me."(The 'ulama differ in their opinions as to the meaning of the word "we". Some say it is Messenger of Allah ﷺ himself, and the plural is used as a mark of respect. Others explain that it is the ambiyaa (prophets). According to some it is Messenger of Allah ﷺ and his relatives, for whom it is not permissible to accept zakaah. According to this humble servant the third ihtimaal (supposition) is superior and more acceptable. Allaamah Munaawi's criticism of the third explanation is not forceful and weighty). On the next day this happened again. Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) brought a tray of fresh dates, and in reply to the question of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he replied: "O messenger of Allah, it is a present for you".Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to the Sahaabah (may Allah be pleased with him)m "Help yourselves". (Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ himself ate from it. Bayjuri explains this thus: Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) bringing the dates on both days in this manner was to investigate, and to make Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ his master. Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) was an 'Aalim (learned) of the old days. He lived for a hundred and fifty years and according to some, he lived three hundred years. He had seen the signs of Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ in the kitaabs of previous prophets, that he will not accept sadaqah, but shall accept presents and gifts, and the seal of Prophethood will be between his two shoulders after witnessing the first two signs).He then saw the seal of Prophethood on the back of Sayyidina Messenger of Allah ﷺ and embraced Islam. (At that time Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) was a slave of a Jew from the tribe of Banu Qurayzah.Messenger of Allah ﷺ purchased him(this is figuratively speaking. The fact is that Messenger of Allah ﷺ made him a Mukaatab - One who buys One's freedom for any agreed sum.) and paid Dirhams for him to become a Mukaatab, and also agreed that he(Sayyidina Salmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) should plant for the Jew date palms,(the amount of three hundred palms) and until these bore fruit to tend them. Messenger of Allah ﷺ planted the palms with his mubaarak hands and it was his mu'jizah(miracle) that all the palms bore fruit in the same year. One tree among these did not bear fruit. Upon investigating it was found that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) had planted this tree, and that it was not planted by Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ removed this palm and replanted it. Another mu'jizah Messenger of Allah ﷺ is that he planted the palms out of season and they bore fruit the same year.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَمَّارٍ الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ حُرَيْثٍ الْخُزَاعِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ وَاقِدٍ، حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ بُرَيْدَةَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبِي بُرَيْدَةَ، يَقُولُ: جَاءَ سَلْمَانُ الْفَارِسِيُّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، حِينَ قَدِمَ الْمَدِينَةَ بِمَائِدَةٍ عَلَيْهَا رُطَبٌ، فَوَضَعَهَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، فَقَالَ: يَا سَلْمَانُ مَا هَذَا؟ فَقَالَ: صَدَقَةٌ عَلَيْكَ، وَعَلَى أَصْحَابِكَ، فَقَالَ: ارْفَعْهَا، فَإِنَّا لا نَأْكُلُ الصَّدَقَةَ، قَالَ: فَرَفَعَهَا، فَجَاءَ الْغَدَ بِمِثْلِهِ، فَوَضَعَهُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، فَقَالَ: مَا هَذَا يَا سَلْمَانُ؟ فَقَالَ: هَدِيَّةٌ لَكَ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ لأَصْحَابِهِ: ابْسُطُوا ثُمَّ نَظَرَ إِلَى الْخَاتَمِ عَلَى ظَهْرِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، فَآمَنَ بِهِ، وَكَانَ لِلْيَهُودِ فَاشْتَرَاهُ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، بِكَذَا وَكَذَا دِرْهَمًا عَلَى أَنْ يَغْرِسَ لَهُمْ نَخْلا، فَيَعْمَلَ سَلْمَانُ فِيهِ، حَتَّى تُطْعِمَ، فَغَرَسَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، النَّخلَ إِلا نَخْلَةً وَاحِدَةً، غَرَسَهَا عُمَرُ فَحَمَلَتِ النَّخْلُ مِنْ عَامِهَا، وَلَمْ تَحْمِلْ نَخْلَةٌ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ: مَا شَأْنُ هَذِهِ النَّخْلَةِ؟ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، أَنَا غَرَسْتُهَا، فَنَزَعَهَا رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، فَغَرَسَهَا فَحَمَلَتْ مِنْ عَامِهَا.
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 20

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 21
englishRaw:
Abi Nadrah AI-'Awfee RA. reports:
                      "I asked Abu Sa'eed Khudari (may Allah be pleased with him) about the Seal of Prophethood of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said: 'It was a piece of raised flesh that was on Messenger of Allah ﷺ's back".
narrator: Abi Nadrah AI-'Awfee RA. reports:
body: "I asked Abu Sa'eed Khudari (may Allah be pleased with him) about the Seal of Prophethood of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said: 'It was a piece of raised flesh that was on Messenger of Allah ﷺ's back".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بِشْرُ بْنُ الْوَضَّاحِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَقِيلٍ الدَّوْرَقِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي نَضْرَةَ الْعَوَقِيِّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيَّ، عَنْ خَاتَمِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ يَعْنِي خَاتَمَ النُّبُوَّةِ، فَقَالَ: كَانَ فِي ظَهْرِهِ بَضْعَةٌ نَاشِزَةٌ.
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 21

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 22
englishRaw:
Abdullah bin Sarjas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I came to Messenger of Allah ﷺ while there were people sitting in his company. I went around to the back of Messenger of Allah ﷺ (The narrator may have done this physically). Messenger of Allah ﷺ understood what I was trying to do. He removed the sheet (body wrap) from his back. I saw the place of the Seal of Prophethood between his two shoulders. It was like a cluster surrounded by til (moles) which appeared to be like a wart. I came before Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said to him. May Allah forgive you (or Allah has forgiven you, as is mentioned in Surah Fath, 'That Allah may forgive you, your former and your latter sins'). Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: 'May Allah forgive you too'. The people said to me that Messenger of Allah ﷺ has made du'aa for your maghfirah (forgiveness). I replied: 'Yes, and for you too, because Allah Ta'aala has said: 'O Muhammad, seek forgiveness for yourself and the Mu'min males and females also"'. (That is why Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked for the forgiveness of Muslims).
narrator: Abdullah bin Sarjas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I came to Messenger of Allah ﷺ while there were people sitting in his company. I went around to the back of Messenger of Allah ﷺ (The narrator may have done this physically). Messenger of Allah ﷺ understood what I was trying to do. He removed the sheet (body wrap) from his back. I saw the place of the Seal of Prophethood between his two shoulders. It was like a cluster surrounded by til (moles) which appeared to be like a wart. I came before Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said to him. May Allah forgive you (or Allah has forgiven you, as is mentioned in Surah Fath, 'That Allah may forgive you, your former and your latter sins'). Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: 'May Allah forgive you too'. The people said to me that Messenger of Allah ﷺ has made du'aa for your maghfirah (forgiveness). I replied: 'Yes, and for you too, because Allah Ta'aala has said: 'O Muhammad, seek forgiveness for yourself and the Mu'min males and females also"'. (That is why Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked for the forgiveness of Muslims).
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ الْمِقْدَامِ أَبُو الأَشْعَثِ الْعِجْلِيُّ الْبَصْرِيُّ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ الأَحْوَلِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ سَرْجِسَ، قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ وَهُوَ فِي نَاسٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ، فَدُرْتُ هَكَذَا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ، فَعَرَفَ الَّذِي أُرِيدُ، فَأَلْقَى الرِّدَاءَ عَنْ ظَهْرِهِ، فَرَأَيْتُ مَوْضِعَ الْخَاتَمِ عَلَى كَتِفَيْهِ، مِثْلَ الْجُمْعِ حَوْلَهَا خِيلانٌ، كَأَنَّهَا ثَآلِيلُ، فَرَجَعْتُ حَتَّى اسْتَقْبَلْتُهُ، فَقُلْتُ: غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، فَقَالَ: وَلَكَ فَقَالَ الْقَوْمُ: أَسْتَغْفَرَ لَكَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ؟ فَقَالَ: نَعَمْ، وَلَكُمْ، ثُمَّ تَلا هَذِهِ الآيَةَ ?وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنْبِكَ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِنَاتِ?..
references: Book of Seal Of Nubuwwah (Prophethood) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 22

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 23
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported:
                      "The hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ reached till half of his ears".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reported:
body: "The hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ reached till half of his ears".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ شَعَرُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ إِلَى نِصْفِ أُذُنَيْهِ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 23

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 24
englishRaw:
Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reported:
                      Messenger of Allah ﷺ and I bathed in one utensil, and the mubarak hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was longer than those that reached the ear lobes, and were less than those that reached the shoulders". (It means that they were not very long nor were they short, but of a medium length).
narrator: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reported:
body: Messenger of Allah ﷺ and I bathed in one utensil, and the mubarak hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was longer than those that reached the ear lobes, and were less than those that reached the shoulders". (It means that they were not very long nor were they short, but of a medium length).
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّادُ بْنُ السَّرِيِّ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي الزِّنَادِ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كُنْتُ أَغْتَسِلُ أَنَا وَرَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ مِنْ إِنَاءٍ وَاحِدٍ، وَكَانَ لَهُ شَعَرٌ فَوْقَ الْجُمَّةِ، وَدُونَ الْوَفْرَةِ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 24

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 25
englishRaw:
Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was of average hight, and the portion between the two shoulders was wide. His hair was till his ear lobes".
narrator: Baraa bin Aazib (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was of average hight, and the portion between the two shoulders was wide. His hair was till his ear lobes".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو قَطَنٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ عَازِبٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ مَرْبُوعًا، بَعِيدَ مَا بَيْنَ الْمِنْكَبَيْنِ، وَكَانَتْ جُمَّتُهُ تَضْرِبُ شَحْمَةَ أُذُنَيْهِ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 25

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 26
englishRaw:
Qataadah bin Da'aamah As-Sadusi relates:
                      "I asked Anas R.A., 'How was the hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ?'. He replied: 'It was not very twisted, nor very straight. It had a slight twist and was a bit curled, and reached till his ear-lobes'".
narrator: Qataadah bin Da'aamah As-Sadusi relates:
body: "I asked Anas R.A., 'How was the hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ?'. He replied: 'It was not very twisted, nor very straight. It had a slight twist and was a bit curled, and reached till his ear-lobes'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَهْبُ بْنُ جَرِيرِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لأَنَسٍ: كَيْفَ كَانَ شَعَرُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ؟ قَالَ: لَمْ يَكُنْ بِالْجَعْدِ، وَلا بِالسَّبْطِ، كَانَ يَبْلُغُ شَعَرُهُ شَحْمَةَ أُذُنَيْهِ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 26

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 27
englishRaw:
Umme Haani bint Abi Taalib (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Makkah once after the hijrah. His mubarak hair had four plaits".
narrator: Umme Haani bint Abi Taalib (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Makkah once after the hijrah. His mubarak hair had four plaits".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي عُمَرَ الْمَكِّيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ أُمِّ هَانِئٍ بِنْتِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، قَالَتْ: قَدِمَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ مَكَّةَ قَدْمَةً، وَلَهُ أَرْبَعُ غَدَائِرَ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 27

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 28
englishRaw: It is reported from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ reached till the middle of the ears.
body: It is reported from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ reached till the middle of the ears.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُوَيْدُ بْنُ نَصْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ الْبُنَانِيِّ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ: أَنَّ شَعَرَ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، كَانَ إِلَى أَنْصَافِ أُذُنَيْهِ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 28

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 29
englishRaw:
Ibne Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to leave his hair the way it naturally was, without making a path in the hair (parting hair). The reason being that the mushrikeen (polytheists) used to make a path in their hair, and the Ahlul Kitaab (People of the Book) did not do so. In the early periods Messenger of Allah ﷺ preferred to follow the Ahlul Kitaab, rather than others, in matters where no command had came from Allah. Later this was abrogated, and Messenger of Allah ﷺ began apposing the ways of the Ahlul Kitaab after this".
narrator: Ibne Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to leave his hair the way it naturally was, without making a path in the hair (parting hair). The reason being that the mushrikeen (polytheists) used to make a path in their hair, and the Ahlul Kitaab (People of the Book) did not do so. In the early periods Messenger of Allah ﷺ preferred to follow the Ahlul Kitaab, rather than others, in matters where no command had came from Allah. Later this was abrogated, and Messenger of Allah ﷺ began apposing the ways of the Ahlul Kitaab after this".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُوَيْدُ بْنُ نَصْرٍ نُ للّعَبْدِ اَهِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ كَانَ يُسْدِلُ شَعَرَهُ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللهِ بْوَكَانَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ يَفْرِقُونَ رُؤُوسَهُمْ، وَكَانَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ يُسْدِلُونَ رُؤُوسَهُمْ، وَكَانَ يُحِبُّ مُوَافَقَةَ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ فِيمَا لَمْ يُؤْمَرْ فِيهِ بِشَيْءٍ، ثُمَّ فَرَقَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ رَأْسَهُ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 29

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 30
englishRaw:
Umme Haani (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ with four side locks (on his hair)".Apparently this is the same hadith as has been quoted earlier.
narrator: Umme Haani (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ with four side locks (on his hair)".Apparently this is the same hadith as has been quoted earlier.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ نَافِعٍ الْمَكِّيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ أُمِّ هَانِئٍ، قَالَتْ: رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ ذَا ضَفَائِرَ أَرْبَعٍ.
references: Book of The Mubarak Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 30

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 31
englishRaw: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says, "I used to comb the hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, even when I was in the state of menstruation."
body: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says, "I used to comb the hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, even when I was in the state of menstruation."
arabic: حدثنا إسحاق بن موسى الأنصاري، حدثنا مَعْن بن عيسى، حدثنا مالك بن أنس، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كُنْتُ أُرَجِّلُ رَأْسَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَأَنَا حَائِضٌ.
references: Book of The Combing Of The Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 31

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 32
englishRaw: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ often rubbed oil in his head and also often combed his beard. He put a cloth over his head, which became like an oil cloth due to the frequent use of oil."
body: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ often rubbed oil in his head and also often combed his beard. He put a cloth over his head, which became like an oil cloth due to the frequent use of oil."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يُوسُفُ بْنُ عِيسَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الرَّبِيعُ بْنُ صَبِيحٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبَانَ هُوَ الرَّقَاشِيُّ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يُكْثِرُ دَهْنَ رَأْسِهِ وَتَسْرِيحَ لِحْيَتِهِ، وَيُكْثِرُ الْقِنَاعَ حَتَّى كَأَنَّ ثَوْبَهُ، ثَوْبُ زَيَّاتٍ.
references: Book of The Combing Of The Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 32

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 33
englishRaw: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ liked to begin with the right while combing his hair; while performing ablution (wudhu) and when putting on his shoes.
body: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ liked to begin with the right while combing his hair; while performing ablution (wudhu) and when putting on his shoes.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّادُ بْنُ السَّرِيِّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الأَحْوَصِ، عَنِ الأَشْعَثِ بْنِ أَبِي الشَّعْثَاءِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: إِنْ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ لَيُحِبُّ التَّيَمُّنَ فِي طُهُورِهِ إِذَا تَطَهَّرَ، وَفِي تَرَجُّلِهِ إِذَا تَرَجَّلَ، وَفِي انْتِعَالِهِ إِذَا انْتَعَلَ.
references: Book of The Combing Of The Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 33

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 34
englishRaw: Abdullah Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) reports, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ prohibited the combing of hair, but allowed it to be done occasionally."
body: Abdullah Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) reports, "Messenger of Allah ﷺ prohibited the combing of hair, but allowed it to be done occasionally."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ مُغَفَّلٍ، قَالَ: نَهَى رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ عنِ التَّرَجُّلِ، إِلا غِبًّا.
references: Book of The Combing Of The Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 34

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 35
englishRaw: Humayd bin Abdurrahman (may Allah be pleased with him) relates form a Sahabi that Messenger of Allah ﷺ combed his hair occasionally.
body: Humayd bin Abdurrahman (may Allah be pleased with him) relates form a Sahabi that Messenger of Allah ﷺ combed his hair occasionally.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَرَفَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَلاءِ الأَوْدِيِّ، عَنْ حُمَيْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، كَانَ يَتَرَجَّلُ غِبًّا.
references: Book of The Combing Of The Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 35

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 36
englishRaw:
Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
"I asked Anas (may Allah be pleased with him), 'Did Messenger of Allah ﷺ dye his hair?'" He replied: "The hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not reach such a stage where one would use a dye; however Messenger of Allah (Sallallahu alihe wasallam) did have a few white hair on his temples, on the other hand Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him)dyed his hair with Hina and Katm."
narrator: Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I asked Anas (may Allah be pleased with him), 'Did Messenger of Allah ﷺ dye his hair?'" He replied: "The hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not reach such a stage where one would use a dye; however Messenger of Allah (Sallallahu alihe wasallam) did have a few white hair on his temples, on the other hand Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him)dyed his hair with Hina and Katm."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا هَمَّامٌ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لأَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ: هَلْ خَضَبَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ؟ قَالَ: لَمْ يَبْلُغْ ذَلِكَ، إِنَّمَا كَانَ شَيْبًا فِي صُدْغَيْهِ وَلَكِنْ أَبُو بَكْرٍ، خَضَبَ بِالْحِنَّاءِ وَالْكَتَمِ.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 36

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 37
englishRaw:
Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I did not count more than fourteen white hair on the head and beard of Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
narrator: Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I did not count more than fourteen white hair on the head and beard of Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، وَيَحْيَى بْنُ مُوسَى، قَالا: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: مَا عَدَدْتُ فِي رَأْسِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ وَلِحْيَتِهِ، إِلا أَرْبَعَ عَشْرَةَ شَعَرَةً بَيْضَاءَ.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 37

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 38
englishRaw:
Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him)was asked a question regarding the white hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He replied:
                      "When Messenger of Allah ﷺ oiled his hair, it (white hair) did not show. When he did not oil it, it showed".
narrator: Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him)was asked a question regarding the white hair of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He replied:
body: "When Messenger of Allah ﷺ oiled his hair, it (white hair) did not show. When he did not oil it, it showed".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْبٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ جَابِرَ بْنَ سَمُرَةَ، وَقَدْ سُئِلَ عَنْ شَيْبِ رَسُولِ اللهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّم، فَقَالَ: كَانَ إِذَا دَهَنَ رَأْسَهُ لَمْ يُرَ مِنْهُ شَيْبٌ، وَإِذَا لَمْ يَدْهِنْ رُئِيَ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 38

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 39
englishRaw:
Ibne Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ had about twenty white hair".
narrator: Ibne Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ had about twenty white hair".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ الْكِنْدِيُّ الْكُوفِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ، عَنْ شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: إِنَّمَا كَانَ شَيْبُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ نَحْوًا مِنْ عِشْرِينَ شَعَرَةً بَيْضَاءَ.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 39

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 40
englishRaw:
Ibne Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him)once said, 'O Messenger of Allah, you have become old'. (What is the reason? Due to this mild and soft temperament he ought to be young, and also according to his age he could be still called young). Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: "The recitation of Surahs Hud, Waaqiah, Mursalat, Amma and Kuwwirah has made me old'".
narrator: Ibne Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him)once said, 'O Messenger of Allah, you have become old'. (What is the reason? Due to this mild and soft temperament he ought to be young, and also according to his age he could be still called young). Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: "The recitation of Surahs Hud, Waaqiah, Mursalat, Amma and Kuwwirah has made me old'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْبٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْعَلاءِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ شَيْبَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، قَدْ شِبْتَ، قَالَ: شَيَّبَتْنِي هُودٌ، وَالْوَاقِعَةُ، وَالْمُرْسَلاتُ، وَعَمَّ يَتَسَاءَلُونَ، وَإِذَا الشَّمْسُ كُوِّرَتْ.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 40

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 41
englishRaw:
Abu Juhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      'The people said: "O Rasulallah, ﷺ! we see the signs of old age beginning to show on you?" Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: "Surah Hud and similar surahs have made me old".
narrator: Abu Juhayfa (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: 'The people said: "O Rasulallah, ﷺ! we see the signs of old age beginning to show on you?" Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: "Surah Hud and similar surahs have made me old".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بِشْرٍ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي جُحَيْفَةَ، قَالَ: قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، نَرَاكَ قَدْ شِبْتَ، قَالَ: قَدْ شَيَّبَتْنِي هُودٌ وَأَخَوَاتُهَا.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 41

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 42
englishRaw:
Abu Rimtha Taymi (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I went with my son to Messenger of Allah ﷺ. (Probably he had not see Messenger of Allah ﷺ before). When I saw him I said to myself immediately, 'This is the true Messenger of Allah'. At that time Messenger of Allah ﷺ was wearing two pieces of green clothing. (His lungi-sarong, waist-wrap and shawl were both green). On a few hairs there were signs of old age, but those hair were red".
narrator: Abu Rimtha Taymi (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I went with my son to Messenger of Allah ﷺ. (Probably he had not see Messenger of Allah ﷺ before). When I saw him I said to myself immediately, 'This is the true Messenger of Allah'. At that time Messenger of Allah ﷺ was wearing two pieces of green clothing. (His lungi-sarong, waist-wrap and shawl were both green). On a few hairs there were signs of old age, but those hair were red".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعَيْبُ بْنُ صَفْوَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، عَنِ إِيَادِ بْنِ لَقِيطٍ الْعِجْلِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي رِمْثَةَ التَّيْمِيِّ، تَيْمِ الرَّبَابِ، قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، وَمَعِي ابْنٌ لِي، قَالَ: فَأَرَيْتُهُ، فَقُلْتُ لَمَّا رَأَيْتُهُ: هَذَا نَبِيُّ اللهِ ﷺ وَعَلَيْهِ ثَوْبَانِ أَخْضَرَانِ، وَلَهُ شَعَرٌ قَدْ عَلاهُ الشَّيْبُ، وَشَيْبُهُ أَحْمَرُ.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 42

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 43
englishRaw:
Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him)was asked:
                      "Were there any white hair in the blessed head of Messenger of Allah ﷺ? He replied: 'He only had a few on the middle parting, and when Rasuullah ﷺ oiled his hair, they did not show'".
narrator: Jaabir bin Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him)was asked:
body: "Were there any white hair in the blessed head of Messenger of Allah ﷺ? He replied: 'He only had a few on the middle parting, and when Rasuullah ﷺ oiled his hair, they did not show'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُرَيْجُ بْنُ النُّعْمَانِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ سِمَاكِ بْنِ حَرْبٍ، قَالَ: قِيلَ لِجَابِرِ بْنِ سَمُرَةَ: أَكَانَ فِي رَأْسِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ شَيْبٌ؟ قَالَ: لَمْ يَكُنْ فِي رَأْسِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، شَيْبٌ إِلا شَعَرَاتٌ فِي مَفْرِقِ رَأْسِهِ، إِذَا ادَّهَنَ وَارَاهُنَّ الدُّهْنُ.
references: Book of Appearing Of The White Hair Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 43

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 44
englishRaw:
Abu Rimthah Taymi (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I attended a gathering of Messenger of Allah ﷺ with my son. Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked me, 'Is this your son?' I replied: 'Yes, this is my son. You be a witness to it.' Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Alahi Wasallam said: 'The revenge of his crime (jinayah) is not on you, nor is the revenge of your crime on him.' (This will be explained in the commentary). Abu Rimthah may Allah be pleased with him says: 'At that time I noticed a few hair of Messenger of Allah Salallahu 'Alayhi Wasallam were red." Imam Tirmidhi says: "This hadith is the most correct and closest on the subject of using a dye."
narrator: Abu Rimthah Taymi (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I attended a gathering of Messenger of Allah ﷺ with my son. Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked me, 'Is this your son?' I replied: 'Yes, this is my son. You be a witness to it.' Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Alahi Wasallam said: 'The revenge of his crime (jinayah) is not on you, nor is the revenge of your crime on him.' (This will be explained in the commentary). Abu Rimthah may Allah be pleased with him says: 'At that time I noticed a few hair of Messenger of Allah Salallahu 'Alayhi Wasallam were red." Imam Tirmidhi says: "This hadith is the most correct and closest on the subject of using a dye."
arabic:
حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ ، قَالَ : حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ ، قَالَ : حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ عُمَيْرٍ ، عَنِ إِيَادِ بْنِ لَقِيطٍ ، قَالَ : أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو رِمْثَةَ ، قَالَ : أَتَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مَعَ ابْنٍ لِي ، فَقَالَ : " ابْنُكَ هَذَا ؟ " فَقُلْتُ : نَعَمْ , أَشْهَدُ بِهِ ، قَالَ : " لا يَجْنِي عَلَيْكَ ، وَلا تَجْنِي عَلَيْهِ " ، قَالَ : وَرَأَيْتُ الشَّيْبَ أَحْمَرَ ، 

قَالَ أَبُو عِيسَى : هَذَا أَحْسَنُ شَيْءٍ رُوِيَ فِي هَذَا الْبَابِ ، وَأَفْسَرُ لأَنَّ الرِّوَايَاتِ الصَّحِيحَةَ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَمْ يَبْلُغِ الشَّيْبَ . وَأَبُو رِمْثَةَ اسْمُهُ : رِفَاعَةُ بْنُ يَثْرِبِيٍّ التَّيْمِيُّ .
references: Book of Prophet of Allah ﷺ Using a Dye, Hadith 44

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 45
englishRaw:
Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) was asked:
                      "Did Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam use a dye?" (for his hair). He replied: "Yes."
narrator: Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) was asked:
body: "Did Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam use a dye?" (for his hair). He replied: "Yes."
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، عَنْ شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ مَوْهَبٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ: هَلْ خَضَبَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ.
references: Book of Prophet of Allah ﷺ Using a Dye, Hadith 45

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 46
englishRaw:
Jah-dhamah (may Allah be pleased with her), the wife of Bashir Kha-saasiyyah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ coming out of the house after taking a bath and was stroking (or combing) his hair. There were signs of henna on his mubaarak head."
narrator: Jah-dhamah (may Allah be pleased with her), the wife of Bashir Kha-saasiyyah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ coming out of the house after taking a bath and was stroking (or combing) his hair. There were signs of henna on his mubaarak head."
