Mulla 'Ali Qaari has written that according to the madh-hab of Imaam Aa'zam Abu Hanifah, in tahajjud prayers there are twelve rak'ahs). He then performed the witr salaah and slept. When the mu-adh-dhin (Sayyidina Bilaal (may Allah be pleased with him)) came to him, he got up and recited two short rak'ahs and went for the fajr salaah.
narrator: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that he once slept at the house of his aunt Maymunah (during his childhood). She slept on the width of the cushion and Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Alayhi Wasallarn slept on the length of the cushion. (Qaadi 'Iyaad and others have translated pillow as a bed. When the original word means pillow and it is possible to use it in such a manner, it is not necessary to translate it as a bed. For instance, Messenger of Allah Saliallahu 'Alayhi Wasallam must have slept on the length of the pillow facing the qiblah, and Sayyidina Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) on the breadth of it, putting his head on the qiblah side). Messenger of Allah ﷺ (after having a little conversation with his wife) slept till the middle of the night, or till a little before that. Messenger of Allah ﷺ then awakened and began wiping off the signs of sleep from his face. He then recited the last ten aayaat of Surah Aali 'Imraan (Inna fi khalqis samaawaati wal ard). (The 'ulama say a little of the Qur-aan should be recited after awakening, as this creates strength and it is mustahab to recite these aayaat). He got up and went to a leather bag that was hanging and (took water in a utensil from it) performed wudu from it. He then commenced his salaah. 'Abdullah bin 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: 'I also got up (performed wudu) and stood next to-him (on his left). Messenger of Allah ﷺ put his right hand on my head and caught my ear and twisted it (A muqtadi should stand on the right side of an Imaam. The ear was twisted to remind him. In one narration it is stated that, I began to sleep, so Messenger of Allah ﷺ caught my ear. In another narration it is stated he caught my ear and pulled me to his right side, so that I might stand on the right according to the sunnah). He performed two rak'ahs, then two rak'ahs, then two rak'ahs, then two, rak'ahs, then two rak'ahs, then two rak'ahs. Ma'n (a narrator of this hadith) says Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited two two rak'ahs six times (the total of twelve rak'ahs. Mulla 'Ali Qaari has written that according to the madh-hab of Imaam Aa'zam Abu Hanifah, in tahajjud prayers there are twelve rak'ahs). He then performed the witr salaah and slept. When the mu-adh-dhin (Sayyidina Bilaal (may Allah be pleased with him)) came to him, he got up and recited two short rak'ahs and went for the fajr salaah.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ (ح) وَحَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مُوسَى الأَنْصَارِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنٌ، عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ مَخْرَمَةَ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ كُرَيْبٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّهُ أَخْبَرَهُ، أَنَّهُ بَاتَ عِنْدَ مَيْمُونَةَ وَهِيَ خَالَتُهُ، قَالَ: فَاضْطَجَعْتُ فِي عَرْضِ الْوِسَادَةِ، وَاضْطَجَعَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ فِي طُولِهَا، فَنَامَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، حَتَّى إِذَا انْتَصَفَ اللَّيْلُ أَوْ قَبْلَهُ بِقَلِيلٍ أَوْ بَعْدَهُ بِقَلِيلٍ، فَاسْتَيْقَظَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، فَجَعَلَ يَمْسَحُ النَّوْمَ عَنْ وَجْهِهِ، ثُمَّ قَرَأَ الْعَشْرَ الآيَاتِ الْخَوَاتِيمَ مِنْ سُورَةِ آلِ عِمْرَانَ، ثُمَّ قَامَ إِلَى شَنٍّ مُعَلَّقٍ فَتَوَضَّأَ مِنْهَا، فَأَحْسَنَ الْوُضُوءَ، ثُمَّ قَامَ يُصَلِّي، قَالَ عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: فَقُمْتُ إِلَى جَنْبِهِ فَوَضَعَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يَدَهُ الْيُمْنَى عَلَى رَأْسِي ثُمَّ أَخَذَ بِأُذُنِي الْيُمْنَى، فَفَتَلَهَا فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، قَالَ مَعْنٌ: سِتَّ مَرَّاتٍ ثُمَّ أَوْتَرَ، ثُمَّ اضْطَجَعَ حَتَّى جَاءَهُ الْمُؤَذِّنُ، فَقَامَ فَصَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَصَلَّى الصُّبْحَ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 252

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 253
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ sometimes performed 13 rak'ahs of tahajjud (including witr)".
narrator: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ sometimes performed 13 rak'ahs of tahajjud (including witr)".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو كُرَيْبٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْعَلاءِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي جَمْرَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يُصَلِّي مِنَ اللَّيْلِ ثَلاثَ عَشْرَةَ رَكْعَةً.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 253

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 254
englishRaw: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that whenever Messenger of Allah ﷺ could not perform the tahajjud because of some reason. He performed twelve rak'ahs in the day (at the time of chaasht-before midday).
body: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that whenever Messenger of Allah ﷺ could not perform the tahajjud because of some reason. He performed twelve rak'ahs in the day (at the time of chaasht-before midday).
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَوَانَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ زُرَارَةَ بْنِ أَوْفَى، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ كَانَ إِذَا لَمْ يُصَلِّ بِاللَّيْلِ، مَنَعَهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ النَّوْمُ، أَوْ غَلَبَتْهُ عَيْنَاهُ، صَلَّى مِنَ النَّهَارِ ثِنْتَيْ عَشْرَةَ رَكْعَةً.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 254

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 255
englishRaw:
Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says that Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
                      "When one awakens at night (for-tahajjud salaah), two short rak'ahs should, be performed at the beginning"
narrator: Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says that Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
body: "When one awakens at night (for-tahajjud salaah), two short rak'ahs should, be performed at the beginning"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْعَلاءِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ يَعْنِي ابْنَ حَسَّانَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، قَالَ: إِذَا قَامَ أَحَدُكُمْ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَلْيَفْتَتِحْ صَلاتَهُ بِرَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 255

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 256
englishRaw:
Zayd bin Khaalid Al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I once made up my mind that today I will closely study how Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed his prayers. I lay down on the threshold of the house or a tent of Messenger of Allah ﷺ (so that I may have a chance to observe with close attention). Messenger of Allah ﷺ first performed two short rak'ahs. After that he performed long, long, long rak'ahs. (Long is mentioned thrice because of the lengthy periods spent in the rak'ahs). He then performed two rak'ahs shorter than the previous one's. Then performed two rak'ahs shorter than that. And again performed two rak'ahs shorter than the previous one. He again performed two rak'ahs shorter than that. He then performed the witr. All these (amounted to) thirteen rak'ahs"
narrator: Zayd bin Khaalid Al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I once made up my mind that today I will closely study how Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed his prayers. I lay down on the threshold of the house or a tent of Messenger of Allah ﷺ (so that I may have a chance to observe with close attention). Messenger of Allah ﷺ first performed two short rak'ahs. After that he performed long, long, long rak'ahs. (Long is mentioned thrice because of the lengthy periods spent in the rak'ahs). He then performed two rak'ahs shorter than the previous one's. Then performed two rak'ahs shorter than that. And again performed two rak'ahs shorter than the previous one. He again performed two rak'ahs shorter than that. He then performed the witr. All these (amounted to) thirteen rak'ahs"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ (ح) وَحَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مُوسَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللهِ بْنَ قَيْسِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَةَ أَخْبَرَهُ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ الْجُهَنِيِّ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: لأَرْمُقَنَّ صَلاةَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَتَوَسَّدْتُ عَتَبَتَهُ، أَوْ فُسْطَاطَهُ فَصَلَّى رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، رَكْعَتَيْنِ خَفِيفَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ طَوِيلَتَيْنِ، طَوِيلَتَيْنِ، طَوِيلَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَهُمَا دَونَ اللَّتَيْنِ قَبْلَهُمَا، ثُمَّ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَهُمَا دُونَ اللَّتَيْنِ قَبْلَهُمَا، ثُمَّ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَهُمَا دُونَ اللَّتَيْنِ قَبْلَهُمَا، ثُمَّ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَهُمَا دُونَ اللَّتَيْنِ قَبْلَهُمَا، ثُمَّ أَوْتَرَ فَذَلِكَ ثَلاثَ عَشْرَةَ رَكْعَةً.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 256

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 257
englishRaw:
Abi Salamah bin 'Abdurrahmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) says he asked 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) "How was the salaah (how many rak'ah tahajjud was performed) of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in Ramadaan? "She replied:
"Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not perform more than eleven rak'ahs during Ramadhaan or after Ramadhaan. (Eight rak'ahs tahajjud and three rak'ahs witr). He performed four rak'ahs. Do not ask of its length or how wonderfully (i.e. with humbleness and concentration) it was performed. In the same manner he performed four more rak'ahs. In the same manner he again performed four rak'ahs, and do not ask of its length or how wonderfully it was performed. After that he performed three rakahs witr". Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says "I said: "O Messenger of Allah. Do you sleep before you perform witr?" He replied: "O Aisha, my eyes sleep, but my heart remains awake". (This is a special gift to the ambiyah (prophets) that their hearts remain awake at all times)
narrator: Abi Salamah bin 'Abdurrahmaan (may Allah be pleased with him) says he asked 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) "How was the salaah (how many rak'ah tahajjud was performed) of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in Ramadaan? "She replied:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not perform more than eleven rak'ahs during Ramadhaan or after Ramadhaan. (Eight rak'ahs tahajjud and three rak'ahs witr). He performed four rak'ahs. Do not ask of its length or how wonderfully (i.e. with humbleness and concentration) it was performed. In the same manner he performed four more rak'ahs. In the same manner he again performed four rak'ahs, and do not ask of its length or how wonderfully it was performed. After that he performed three rakahs witr". Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says "I said: "O Messenger of Allah. Do you sleep before you perform witr?" He replied: "O Aisha, my eyes sleep, but my heart remains awake". (This is a special gift to the ambiyah (prophets) that their hearts remain awake at all times)
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مُوسَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْمَقْبُرِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، أَنَّهُ أَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ عَائِشَةَ، كَيْفَ كَانَتْ صَلاةُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ فِي رَمَضَانَ؟ فَقَالَتْ: مَا كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ لِيَزِيدَ فِي رَمَضَانَ وَلا فِي غَيْرِهِ عَلَى إِحْدَى عَشْرَةَ رَكْعَةً، يُصَلِّي أَرْبَعًا، لا تَسْأَلْ عَنْ حُسْنِهِنَّ، وَطُولِهِنَّ، ثُمَّ يُصَلِّي أَرْبَعًا لا تَسْأَلْ عَنْ حُسْنِهِنَّ وَطُولِهِنَّ، ثُمَّ يُصَلِّي ثَلاثًا، قَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، أَتَنَامُ قَبْلَ أَنْ تُوتِرَ؟ فَقَالَ: يَا عَائِشَةُ، إِنَّ عَيْنَيَّ تَنَامَانِ، وَلا يَنَامُ قَلْبِي.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 257

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 258
englishRaw: 'Aayesh (may Allah be pleased with her) say "Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam performed eleven rak'ahs at night, of which one was a rak'ah of witr. When he completed this he slept on the right side"
body: 'Aayesh (may Allah be pleased with her) say "Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam performed eleven rak'ahs at night, of which one was a rak'ah of witr. When he completed this he slept on the right side"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مُوسَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، كَانَ يُصَلِّي مِنَ اللَّيْلِ إِحْدَى عَشْرَةَ رَكْعَةً، يُوتِرُ مِنْهَا بِوَاحِدَةٍ، فَإِذَا فَرَغَ مِنْهَا، اضْطَجَعَ عَلَى شِقِّهِ الأَيْمَنِ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 258

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 259
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed nine rak'ahs at night"
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed nine rak'ahs at night"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّادٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الأَحْوَصِ، عَنِ الأَعْمَشِ، عَنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يُصَلِّي مِنَ اللَّيْلِ تِسْعَ رَكَعَاتٍ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 259

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 260
englishRaw:
Hudhayfah bin Al Yamaan (may Allah be pleased with him) says he performed salaah with Messenger of Allah ﷺ one night. (It has been reported in a few narrations that this incident took place during a night of Ramadaan. It is possible that this was tahajjud or Taraweeh salaah). After commencing the salaah he recited:
                     Allahu Akbar, dhul malakuti wal jabaruti wal kibri-yaa-i wal a-za-mati.Translation: Allah is supreme! Lord of Dominion, Power, Majesty, and Magnificence.He then recited (after the Faatihah) Surah Baqarah, and performed ruku'. The length of the ruku' was as long as the qiyaam (standing posture). (The 'ulama give two meanings to this and both are possible. The first is that the ruku' was as long as the qiyaam. For example, if the qiyaam was for one hour, the ruku' was also for about one hour. Accordingly, this mas-alah is established, that if the ruku' and sajdah are longer than normal, the salaah will be valid. The second is that the qiyaam was longer than normal. In this instance if the qiyaam was for an hour, and the ruku' for fifteen minutes, then too it will be truly explaining this hadith. According to this saying the salaah remains in the normal manner. i.e. a fundamental action of salaah that was long, like qiyaam, remained long. And those that were short, like ruku' or sajdah, remained short. But certainly every fundamental action was longer than normal). Messenger of Allah ﷺ repeated:Subhaana rabbiyal azimi-Subhaana rabbiyal azimi.Translation: Glory be to my Lord the Magnificient.He lifted his head from the ruku' and stood. This standing was also long like that of the ruku'. At this moment he repeated:Li rabbiyal hamdu-Li rabbiyal hamdu.Translation: All praises are for my Lord. All praises are for my Lord.He then performed the sajdah. The sajdah was as long as the qawmah (standing in between the ruku' and sajdah). and he recited:Subhaana rabbiyal aa'laa-Subhaana rabbiyal aa'laa.Translation: Glory be to my Lord the Exalted. Glory be to my Lord the Exalted.He then sat up from the sajdah. This sitting was also long as that of the sajdah. Here he repeated:Rabbigh-fir lee-Rabbig-fir lee.Translation: O Lord forgive me. O Lord forgive me.Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited in this salaah Surah Baqarah, Surah Aali 'Imraan, Surah Nisaa, Surah Maa-idah or Surah An'aam. The narrator (Sayyidina Shu'ba (may Allah be pleased with him)) is in doubt regarding the last two surahs, whether is it Maa-idah or An'aam.
narrator: Hudhayfah bin Al Yamaan (may Allah be pleased with him) says he performed salaah with Messenger of Allah ﷺ one night. (It has been reported in a few narrations that this incident took place during a night of Ramadaan. It is possible that this was tahajjud or Taraweeh salaah). After commencing the salaah he recited:
body: Allahu Akbar, dhul malakuti wal jabaruti wal kibri-yaa-i wal a-za-mati.Translation: Allah is supreme! Lord of Dominion, Power, Majesty, and Magnificence.He then recited (after the Faatihah) Surah Baqarah, and performed ruku'. The length of the ruku' was as long as the qiyaam (standing posture). (The 'ulama give two meanings to this and both are possible. The first is that the ruku' was as long as the qiyaam. For example, if the qiyaam was for one hour, the ruku' was also for about one hour. Accordingly, this mas-alah is established, that if the ruku' and sajdah are longer than normal, the salaah will be valid. The second is that the qiyaam was longer than normal. In this instance if the qiyaam was for an hour, and the ruku' for fifteen minutes, then too it will be truly explaining this hadith. According to this saying the salaah remains in the normal manner. i.e. a fundamental action of salaah that was long, like qiyaam, remained long. And those that were short, like ruku' or sajdah, remained short. But certainly every fundamental action was longer than normal). Messenger of Allah ﷺ repeated:Subhaana rabbiyal azimi-Subhaana rabbiyal azimi.Translation: Glory be to my Lord the Magnificient.He lifted his head from the ruku' and stood. This standing was also long like that of the ruku'. At this moment he repeated:Li rabbiyal hamdu-Li rabbiyal hamdu.Translation: All praises are for my Lord. All praises are for my Lord.He then performed the sajdah. The sajdah was as long as the qawmah (standing in between the ruku' and sajdah). and he recited:Subhaana rabbiyal aa'laa-Subhaana rabbiyal aa'laa.Translation: Glory be to my Lord the Exalted. Glory be to my Lord the Exalted.He then sat up from the sajdah. This sitting was also long as that of the sajdah. Here he repeated:Rabbigh-fir lee-Rabbig-fir lee.Translation: O Lord forgive me. O Lord forgive me.Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited in this salaah Surah Baqarah, Surah Aali 'Imraan, Surah Nisaa, Surah Maa-idah or Surah An'aam. The narrator (Sayyidina Shu'ba (may Allah be pleased with him)) is in doubt regarding the last two surahs, whether is it Maa-idah or An'aam.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ، رَجُلٍ مِنَ الأَنْصَارِ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ بَنِي عَبْسٍ، عَنْ حُذَيْفَةَ بْنِ الْيَمَانِ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ، قَالَ: فَلَمَّا دَخَلَ فِي الصَّلاةِ، قَالَ: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ ذُو الْمَلَكُوتِ وَالْجَبَرُوتِ، وَالْكِبْرِيَاءِ وَالْعَظَمَةِ، قَالَ: ثُمَّ قَرَأَ الْبَقَرَةَ، ثُمَّ رَكَعَ رُكُوعَهُ نَحْوًا مِنْ قِيَامِهِ، وَكَانَ يَقُولُ: سُبْحَانَ رَبِّيَ الْعَظِيمِ، سُبْحَانَ رَبِّيَ الْعَظِيمِ ثُمَّ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ، فَكَانَ قِيَامُهُ نَحْوًا مِنْ رُكُوعِهِ، وَكَانَ يَقُولُ: لِرَبِّيَ الْحَمْدُ، لِرَبِّيَ الْحَمْدُ ثُمَّ سَجَدَ، فَكَانَ سُجُودُهُ نَحْوًا مِنْ قِيَامِهِ، وَكَانَ يَقُولُ: سُبْحَانَ رَبِّيَ الأَعْلَى، سُبْحَانَ رَبِّيَ الأَعْلَى ثُمَّ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ، فَكَانَ مَا بَيْنَ السَّجْدَتَيْنِ نَحْوًا مِنَ السُّجُودِ، وَكَانَ يَقُولُ: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي، رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي حَتَّى قَرَأَ الْبَقَرَةَ، وَآلَ عِمْرَانَ، وَالنِّسَاءَ، وَالْمَائِدَةَ، أَوِ الأَنْعَامَ، شُعْبَةُ الَّذِي شَكَّ فِي الْمَائِدَةِ، وَالأَنْعَامِ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 260

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 261
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
                      "Once at night (salaah time), Messenger of Allah ﷺ kept on repeating one aayah.
Commentary: The aayah is the last aayah of Surah Maa-idah:In tu-adh-dhib-hum fa-innahum 'ibaaduk. Wa in tagh-fir lahum fa-innaka antal azizul hakim.Translation: "If Thou punish them, lo! they are Thy slaves, and if Thou forgive them, (lo! the are Thy slaves).Lo! Thou, only Thou, art Mighty, the wise".Surah Maa-idah, 118.The Being that possesses great power, if He wills, He may forgive the criminal. The One that is all Wise, there is Wisdom and benefit in every act of His. The reason for Messenger of Allah ﷺ reciting this aayah while standing and also in the ruku and sajdah, and repeating it many times, is to bring to mind the two attributes of Allah, namely the attributes of justice and forgiveness. The whole scene on the day of qiyaamah will be of these two things. It is stated that Imaam Aa'zam Abu Hanifah RA. also recited the following aayah the whole night:Wamtaazul yauma ayyuhal mujrimun.Translation: "But avaunt ye, O ye guilty, this day!" Surah Yaseen, 58.In this aayah too the scene of qiyaamah is portrayed. That today the sinners should separate and distinguish themselves. What a severe and nerve-chilling command this is. Today they are together with the pious and holy people and benefiting from the barakaat (blessings) they receive, but at this moment the sinners will be separated from them. May the Most Merciful Allah with His Grace keep those pious souls under His shadow, otherwise it is really a time for great trials.
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
body:
"Once at night (salaah time), Messenger of Allah ﷺ kept on repeating one aayah.
Commentary: The aayah is the last aayah of Surah Maa-idah:In tu-adh-dhib-hum fa-innahum 'ibaaduk. Wa in tagh-fir lahum fa-innaka antal azizul hakim.Translation: "If Thou punish them, lo! they are Thy slaves, and if Thou forgive them, (lo! the are Thy slaves).Lo! Thou, only Thou, art Mighty, the wise".Surah Maa-idah, 118.The Being that possesses great power, if He wills, He may forgive the criminal. The One that is all Wise, there is Wisdom and benefit in every act of His. The reason for Messenger of Allah ﷺ reciting this aayah while standing and also in the ruku and sajdah, and repeating it many times, is to bring to mind the two attributes of Allah, namely the attributes of justice and forgiveness. The whole scene on the day of qiyaamah will be of these two things. It is stated that Imaam Aa'zam Abu Hanifah RA. also recited the following aayah the whole night:Wamtaazul yauma ayyuhal mujrimun.Translation: "But avaunt ye, O ye guilty, this day!" Surah Yaseen, 58.In this aayah too the scene of qiyaamah is portrayed. That today the sinners should separate and distinguish themselves. What a severe and nerve-chilling command this is. Today they are together with the pious and holy people and benefiting from the barakaat (blessings) they receive, but at this moment the sinners will be separated from them. May the Most Merciful Allah with His Grace keep those pious souls under His shadow, otherwise it is really a time for great trials.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ نَافِعٍ الْبَصْرِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْوَارِثِ، عَنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ الْعَبْدِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمُتَوَكِّلِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: قَامَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ بِآيَةٍ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ لَيْلَةً.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 261

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 262
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Once at night I performed salaah with Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood for such a long time that I intended to commit an evil deed". Someone asked him what deed did you intend to commit? He replied. "To sit down and leave Messenger of Allah ﷺ alone".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Once at night I performed salaah with Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood for such a long time that I intended to commit an evil deed". Someone asked him what deed did you intend to commit? He replied. "To sit down and leave Messenger of Allah ﷺ alone".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنِ الأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ لَيْلَةً مَعَ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ قَائِمًا حَتَّى هَمَمْتُ بِأَمْرِ سُوءٍ قِيلَ لَهُ: وَمَا هَمَمْتَ بِهِ؟ قَالَ: هَمَمْتُ أَنْ أَقْعُدَ وَأَدَعَ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 262

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 263
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ Performed salaah (in old age) in a sitting posture (due to reciting lengthy portions) When about thirty or forty aayaat were left he stood up and recited (completed) these. He then performed the ruku' and sajdah. He did the same in the second rak'ah".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ Performed salaah (in old age) in a sitting posture (due to reciting lengthy portions) When about thirty or forty aayaat were left he stood up and recited (completed) these. He then performed the ruku' and sajdah. He did the same in the second rak'ah".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مُوسَى الأَنْصَارِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكٌ، عَنْ أَبِي النَّضْرِ، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ كَانَ يُصَلِّي جَالِسًا، فَيَقْرَأُ وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ، فَإِذَا بَقِيَ مِنْ قِرَاءَتِهِ قَدْرُ مَا يَكُونُ ثَلاثِينَ أَوْ أَرْبَعِينَ آيَةً، قَامَ فَقَرَأَ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ، ثُمَّ رَكَعَ وَسَجَدَ، ثُمَّ صَنَعَ فِي الرَّكْعَةِ الثَّانِيَةِ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 263

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 264
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him)' 'Anhu reports that he asked 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) regarding the nawaafil prayers. She replied:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed nawaafil in the long part of the night whilst standing, and performed nawaafil in the long part of the night whilst sitting. His noble habit was that if he stood and recited (the Qur-aan) he performed ruku' and sajdah in a standing posture. If he recited whilst sitting, he performed the ruku' and sajdah in a sitting posture".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him)' 'Anhu reports that he asked 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) regarding the nawaafil prayers. She replied:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed nawaafil in the long part of the night whilst standing, and performed nawaafil in the long part of the night whilst sitting. His noble habit was that if he stood and recited (the Qur-aan) he performed ruku' and sajdah in a standing posture. If he recited whilst sitting, he performed the ruku' and sajdah in a sitting posture".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا خَالِدٌ الْحَذَّاءُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ شَقِيقٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ، عَنْ صَلاةِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، عَنْ تَطَوُّعِهِ، فَقَالَتْ: كَانَ يُصَلِّي لَيْلا طَوِيلا قَائِمًا، وَلَيْلا طَوِيلا قَاعِدًا، فَإِذَا قَرَأَ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ رَكَعَ وَسَجَدَ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ، وَإِذَا قَرَأَ وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ رَكَعَ وَسَجَدَ وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 264

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 265
englishRaw:
Hafsah (may Allah be pleased with her), the wife of Messenger of Allah ﷺ reports:
                      " Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed while sitting. He recited surah in it, and recited with such tarteel (distinct and clear intonation), that the surah became longer than one that is lengthier".
narrator: Hafsah (may Allah be pleased with her), the wife of Messenger of Allah ﷺ reports:
body: " Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed while sitting. He recited surah in it, and recited with such tarteel (distinct and clear intonation), that the surah became longer than one that is lengthier".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مُوسَى الأَنْصَارِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْنٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنِ السَّائِبِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنِ الْمُطَّلِبِ بْنِ أَبِي وَدَاعَةَ، عَنْ حَفْصَةَ، زَوْجِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ يُصَلِّي فِي سُبْحَتِهِ قَاعِدًا، وَيَقْرَأُ بِالسُّورَةِ وَيُرَتِّلُهَا، حَتَّى تَكُونَ أَطْوَلَ مِنْ أَطْوَلَ مِنْهَا.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 265

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 266
englishRaw:
Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed most of his (nafl) salaah in a sitting posture before he passed away".
narrator: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed most of his (nafl) salaah in a sitting posture before he passed away".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الزَّعْفَرَانِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَجَّاجُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي عُثْمَانُ بْنُ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، أَنَّ أَبَا سَلَمَةَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ أَخْبَرَهُ، أَنَّ عَائِشَةَ أَخْبَرَتْهُ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، لَمْ يَمُتْ، حَتَّى كَانَ أَكْثَرُ صَلاتِهِ وَهُوَ جَالِسٌ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 266

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 267
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I performed two rak'ahs before and after zuhr, and two rak'ahs after maghrib with Messenger of Allah ﷺ in his house, and (also) two rak'ahs after 'eshaa in his house".
narrator: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I performed two rak'ahs before and after zuhr, and two rak'ahs after maghrib with Messenger of Allah ﷺ in his house, and (also) two rak'ahs after 'eshaa in his house".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الظُّهْرِ، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَهَا، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْمَغْرِبِ فِي بَيْتِهِ، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعِشَاءِ فِي بَيْتِهِ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 267

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 268
englishRaw: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports.. "Hafsah (my sister, Ummul Mu-mineen) related to me that when the time of fajr began (subh saadiq), and the mu-adh-dhin called out the adhaan, Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed two short rak-'ahs"
body: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports.. "Hafsah (my sister, Ummul Mu-mineen) related to me that when the time of fajr began (subh saadiq), and the mu-adh-dhin called out the adhaan, Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed two short rak-'ahs"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَيُّوبُ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَتْنِي حَفْصَةُ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، كَانَ يُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ حِينَ يَطْلُعُ الْفَجْرُ وَيُنَادِي الْمُنَادِي، قَالَ أَيُّوبُ: وَأُرَاهُ، قَالَ: خَفِيفَتَيْنِ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 268

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 269
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I memorised from Messenger of Allah ﷺ eight raka-aat; two before zuhr and two after zuhr; two rak'ahs after maghrib and two after 'eshaa". Ibn 'Urnar (may Allah be pleased with him) said.. "Hafsah related to me about the two rak'ahs of the morning, which I did not observe from Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
narrator: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I memorised from Messenger of Allah ﷺ eight raka-aat; two before zuhr and two after zuhr; two rak'ahs after maghrib and two after 'eshaa". Ibn 'Urnar (may Allah be pleased with him) said.. "Hafsah related to me about the two rak'ahs of the morning, which I did not observe from Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَرْوَانُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ الْفَزَارِيُّ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بُرْقَانَ، عَنْ مَيْمُونِ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: حَفِظْتُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، ثَمَانِيَ رَكَعَاتٍ: رَكْعَتَيْنِ قَبْلَ الظُّهْرِ، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَهَا، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْمَغْرِبِ، وَرَكْعَتَيْنِ بَعْدَ الْعِشَاءِ، قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ: وَحَدَّثَتْنِي حَفْصَةُ بِرَكْعَتَيِ الْغَدَاةِ، وَلَمْ أَكُنْ أَرَاهُمَا مِنَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 269

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 270
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I asked 'Aisha about the (nawaafil) prayers of Messenger of Allah ﷺ?". She replied: "He used to perform two rak'ahs before and two after zuhr. Two after maghrib and two after 'eshaa, and two before fajr"
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I asked 'Aisha about the (nawaafil) prayers of Messenger of Allah ﷺ?". She replied: "He used to perform two rak'ahs before and two after zuhr. Two after maghrib and two after 'eshaa, and two before fajr"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ يَحْيَى بْنُ خَلَفٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بِشْرُ بْنُ الْمُفَضَّلِ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ الْحَذَّاءِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ شَقِيقٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلتُ عَائِشَةَ، عَنْ صَلاةِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ الظُّهْرِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَبَعْدَهَا رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَبَعْدَ الْمَغْرِبِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَبَعْدَ الْعِشَاءِ رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَقَبْلَ الْفَجْرِ ثِنْتَيْنِ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 270

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 271
englishRaw:
'Aa-sim bin Damrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "We asked'Ali about the nawaafil that Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed in the day". (He must have known already about the nawaafil of the night i.e. tahajjud etc. Many well known narrations have been narrated regarding the tahajiud) 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) replied: "You do not have the strength to perform these." (i.e. The importance, punctuality, humility and humbleness Messenger of Allah ﷺ attached to performing these prayers, cannot be fulfilled. The reason for saying this was to admonish, as what benefit is there simply by asking and investigating, until an effort is not made to practise these) We replied: "The one amongst us who has the strength, will perform it"., (Those who do not possess the strength, will learn so that others could be guided and an effort will be made to practise). 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) said: "In the morning when the sun rises to the height of that, the same as it is at the time for 'asr. At that time Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed two rak'ahs (Salaatul ish-raaq). When the sun rose in the east to the height, where it is in the west at the time of zuhr salaah, he performed four rak'ahs (salaatut duha-chaast, this will be explained in the ensuing chapter). He performed four rak'ahs before the salaah of zuhr, and two after (These six rak'ahs are sunnah mu-akkidah). Four rak'ahs were performed before 'asr. In between the four rak'ahs he sat and sent salutations on the malaa-ikah.muqarrabeen, the ammbiyaa and the mu-mineen"
narrator: 'Aa-sim bin Damrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "We asked'Ali about the nawaafil that Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed in the day". (He must have known already about the nawaafil of the night i.e. tahajjud etc. Many well known narrations have been narrated regarding the tahajiud) 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) replied: "You do not have the strength to perform these." (i.e. The importance, punctuality, humility and humbleness Messenger of Allah ﷺ attached to performing these prayers, cannot be fulfilled. The reason for saying this was to admonish, as what benefit is there simply by asking and investigating, until an effort is not made to practise these) We replied: "The one amongst us who has the strength, will perform it"., (Those who do not possess the strength, will learn so that others could be guided and an effort will be made to practise). 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) said: "In the morning when the sun rises to the height of that, the same as it is at the time for 'asr. At that time Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed two rak'ahs (Salaatul ish-raaq). When the sun rose in the east to the height, where it is in the west at the time of zuhr salaah, he performed four rak'ahs (salaatut duha-chaast, this will be explained in the ensuing chapter). He performed four rak'ahs before the salaah of zuhr, and two after (These six rak'ahs are sunnah mu-akkidah). Four rak'ahs were performed before 'asr. In between the four rak'ahs he sat and sent salutations on the malaa-ikah.muqarrabeen, the ammbiyaa and the mu-mineen"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَاصِمَ بْنَ ضَمْرَةَ، يَقُولُ: سَأَلْنَا عَلِيًّا، عَنْ صَلاةِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ مِنَ النَّهَارِ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّكُمْ لا تُطِيقُونَ ذَلِكَ، قَالَ: فَقُلْنَا: مِنْ أَطَاقَ ذَلِكَ مِنَّا صَلَّى، فَقَالَ: كَانَ إِذَا كَانَتِ الشَّمْسُ مِنْ هَهُنَا كَهَيْئَتِهَا مِنْ هَهُنَا عِنْدَ الْعَصْرِ صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَإِذَا كَانَتِ الشَّمْسُ مِنْ هَهُنَا، كَهَيْئَتِهَا مِنْ هَهُنَا عِنْدَ الظُّهْرِ صَلَّى أَرْبَعًا، وَيُصَلِّي قَبْلَ الظُّهْرِ أَرْبَعًا، وَبَعْدَهَا رَكْعَتَيْنِ، وَقَبْلَ الْعَصْرِ أَرْبَعًا، يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَ كُلِّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ بِالتَّسْلِيمِ عَلَى الْمَلائِكَةِ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ، وَمَنْ تَبِعَهُمْ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُسْلِمِينَ.
references: Book of Worship And Devotion Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 271

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 272
englishRaw:
Mu'aadhah (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
                      "I asked Aisha, 'Did Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform the salaatut duha (chaast prayers) ?" She replied: "Yes, he performed four rak'ahs (the least), and added to it the amount Allah Ta'aala willed".
narrator: Mu'aadhah (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
body: "I asked Aisha, 'Did Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform the salaatut duha (chaast prayers) ?" She replied: "Yes, he performed four rak'ahs (the least), and added to it the amount Allah Ta'aala willed".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ الرِّشْكِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ مُعَاذَةَ، قَالَتْ: قُلْتُ لِعَائِشَةَ: أَكَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يُصَلِّي الضُّحَى؟ قَالَتْ: نَعَمْ، أَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ، وَيَزِيدُ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 272

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 273
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed six rak'ahs of the salaatut duha".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed six rak'ahs of the salaatut duha".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي حَكِيمُ بْنُ مُعَاوِيَةَ الزِّيَادِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زِيَادُ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ اللهِ بْنِ الرَّبِيعِ الزِّيَادِيُّ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ الطَّوِيلِ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، كَانَ يُصَلِّي الضُّحَى سِتَّ رَكَعَاتٍ.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 273

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 274
englishRaw:
'Abdurrahmaan bin Abi Layla RA. (a Taabi'ee) reports:
                      "None had given me the news that they had observed Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform the salaatut duhah besides Ummi Haani. She said: Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to her house, the day Makkah was conquered. He performed ghusl (bath), then performed eight rak'ahs. I did not observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform any salaah shorter than these, despite him performing every ruku' and sajdah properly"'. (He did not perform the ruku' and saidah haphazardly because the salaah was short).
narrator: 'Abdurrahmaan bin Abi Layla RA. (a Taabi'ee) reports:
body: "None had given me the news that they had observed Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform the salaatut duhah besides Ummi Haani. She said: Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to her house, the day Makkah was conquered. He performed ghusl (bath), then performed eight rak'ahs. I did not observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform any salaah shorter than these, despite him performing every ruku' and sajdah properly"'. (He did not perform the ruku' and saidah haphazardly because the salaah was short).
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، قَالَ: مَا أَخْبَرَنِي أَحَدٌ، أَنَّهُ رَأَى النَّبِيَّ ﷺ يُصَلِّي الضُّحَى إِلا أُمُّ هَانِئٍ، فَإِنَّهَا حَدَّثَتْ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، دَخَلَ بَيْتَهَا يَوْمَ فَتْحِ مَكَّةَ فَاغْتَسَلَ فَسَبَّحَ ثَمَانِيَ رَكَعَاتٍ مَا رَأَيْتُهُ ﷺ، صَلَّى صَلاةً قَطُّ أَخَفَّ مِنْهَا، غَيْرَ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُتِمُّ الرُّكُوعَ وَالسُّجُودَ.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 274

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 275
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
                      "I enquired from Aisha, that did Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform the salaatut duha?". She replied: "No he did not regularly perform it, but when he returned from a journey he performed it".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
body: "I enquired from Aisha, that did Messenger of Allah ﷺ perform the salaatut duha?". She replied: "No he did not regularly perform it, but when he returned from a journey he performed it".
arabic: حدثنا ابن أبي عمر , حدثنا وكيع، حدثنا كهمس بن الحسن، عن عبد الله بن شقيق قال: قلت لعائشة: أكان النبي ﷺ يصلي الضحى؟ قالت:لا إلا يجىء من مغيبه.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 275

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 276
englishRaw:
'Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudari (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ at times performed the salaatut duha with such regularity, that we thought, he would not leave it. At times he left it (fearing it might become fard or because of some advantage), till we thought he left it completely, and would not perform it again".
narrator: 'Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudari (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ at times performed the salaatut duha with such regularity, that we thought, he would not leave it. At times he left it (fearing it might become fard or because of some advantage), till we thought he left it completely, and would not perform it again".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زِيَادُ بْنُ أَيُّوبَ الْبَغْدَادِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَبِيعَةَ، عَنْ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ مَرْزُوقٍ، عَنْ عَطِيَّةَ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ، قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ، يُصَلِّي الضُّحَى حَتَّى نَقُولَ: لا يَدَعُهَا، وَيَدَعُهَا حَتَّى نَقُولَ: لا يُصَلِّيهَا.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 276

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 277
englishRaw:
Abu Ayyub Ansaari (may Allah be pleased with him) says. "Messenger of Allah ﷺ always performed four rak'ahs around the time of zawaal. I said:
                      'O Messenger of Allah. You give much importance to these four rak'ahs around the time of zawaal of the sun?' Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: 'The doors of the skies open from the zawaal of the sun till the zuhr salaah is performed. I desire that a good deed of mine reaches the skies at that time'. I asked: 'Is there a recital in every rak'ah?' He replied: 'Yes'. I enquired: 'Should salaam be made at the end of two rak'ahs?' Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied 'No' (Salaam should be made only at the end of four rak'ahs)".
narrator: Abu Ayyub Ansaari (may Allah be pleased with him) says. "Messenger of Allah ﷺ always performed four rak'ahs around the time of zawaal. I said:
body: 'O Messenger of Allah. You give much importance to these four rak'ahs around the time of zawaal of the sun?' Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: 'The doors of the skies open from the zawaal of the sun till the zuhr salaah is performed. I desire that a good deed of mine reaches the skies at that time'. I asked: 'Is there a recital in every rak'ah?' He replied: 'Yes'. I enquired: 'Should salaam be made at the end of two rak'ahs?' Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied 'No' (Salaam should be made only at the end of four rak'ahs)".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ، عَنْ هُشَيْمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدَةُ، عَنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ سَهْمِ بْنِ مِنْجَابٍ، عَنْ قَرْثَعٍ الضَّبِّيِّ، أَوْ عَنْ قَزَعَةَ، عَنْ قَرْثَعٍ، عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ الأَنْصَارِيِّ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، كَانَ يُدْمِنُ أَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ عِنْدَ زَوَالِ الشَّمْسِ، فَقُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، إِنَّكَ تُدْمِنُ هَذِهِ الأَرْبَعَ رَكَعَاتٍ عِنْدَ زَوَالِ الشَّمْسِ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّ أَبْوَابَ السَّمَاءِ تُفْتَحُ عِنْدَ زَوَالِ الشَّمْسِ فَلا تُرْتَجُ حَتَّى تُصَلَّى الظُّهْرُ، فَأُحِبُّ أَنْ يَصْعَدَ لِي فِي تِلْكَ السَّاعَةِ خَيْرٌ، قُلْتُ: أَفِي كُلِّهِنَّ قِرَاءَةٌ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ قُلْتُ: هَلْ فِيهِنَّ تَسْلِيمٌ فَاصِلٌ؟ قَالَ: لا.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 277

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 278
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Saa-ib (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed four rak'ahs after zawaal before the zuhr salaah, and used to say: 'The doors of the heavens open at this moment. I like that a good deed of mine ascend there at this moment'".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Saa-ib (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed four rak'ahs after zawaal before the zuhr salaah, and used to say: 'The doors of the heavens open at this moment. I like that a good deed of mine ascend there at this moment'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ أَبِي الْوَضَّاحِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ الْجَزَرِيِّ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ كَانَ يُصَلِّي أَرْبَعًا بَعْدَ أَنْ تَزُولَ الشَّمْسُ قَبْلَ الظُّهْرِ وَقَالَ: إِنَّهَا سَاعَةٌ تُفْتَحُ فِيهَا أَبْوَابُ السَّمَاءِ، فَأُحِبُّ أَنْ يَصْعَدَ لِي فِيهَا عَمَلٌ صَالِحٌ.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 278

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 279
englishRaw:
'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) used to perform four rak'ahs before zuhr and used to say:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ also performed these four rak'ahs after zawaal, and recited long verses therein".
narrator: 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) used to perform four rak'ahs before zuhr and used to say:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ also performed these four rak'ahs after zawaal, and recited long verses therein".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ يَحْيَى بْنُ خَلَفٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الْمُقَدَّمِيُّ، عَنْ مِسْعَرِ بْنِ كِدَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ ضَمْرَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ الظُّهْرِ أَرْبَعًا، وَذَكَرَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ كَانَ يُصَلِّيهَا عِنْدَ الزَّوَالِ وَيَمُدُّ فِيهَا.
references: Book of Salaatut Duha (Chaast Prayers), Hadith 279

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 280
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I enquired from Messenger of Allah ﷺ, is it more meritorious to perform nafil salaah in the home or in the masjid? Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Aayhi Wasallam replied: 'Do you observe that my house is so near the masjid (which does not make it difficult, or cause a hindrance in coming to the masjid, in spite of all this) I prefer to perform the salaah, besides the faraa-id, in my house, rather than in the masjid".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I enquired from Messenger of Allah ﷺ, is it more meritorious to perform nafil salaah in the home or in the masjid? Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Aayhi Wasallam replied: 'Do you observe that my house is so near the masjid (which does not make it difficult, or cause a hindrance in coming to the masjid, in spite of all this) I prefer to perform the salaah, besides the faraa-id, in my house, rather than in the masjid".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّاسٌ الْعَنْبَرِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنِ الْعَلاءِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ حَرَامِ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَمِّهِ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ عَنِ الصَّلاةِ فِي بَيْتِي وَالصَّلاةِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ، قَالَ: قَدْ تَرَى مَا أَقْرَبَ بَيْتِي مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ، فَلأَنْ أُصَلِّيَ فِي بَيْتِي أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ أَنْ أُصَلِّيَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ، إِلا أَنْ تَكُونَ صَلاةً مَكْتُوبَةً.
references: Book of Prophet of Allah ﷺ Performing Nawaafil At Home, Hadith 280

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 281
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I inquired from 'Aisha Radhiyallahu 'Anha regarding the (nafil) saum of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. She replied: 'At times Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted continuously. We used to say, he will keep on fasting (this month). At times he did not fast, till we began thinking he will not fast (that month). Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not fast for a month after he came to Madinah, besides the fasts of Ramadhaan'".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Shaqeeq (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I inquired from 'Aisha Radhiyallahu 'Anha regarding the (nafil) saum of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. She replied: 'At times Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted continuously. We used to say, he will keep on fasting (this month). At times he did not fast, till we began thinking he will not fast (that month). Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not fast for a month after he came to Madinah, besides the fasts of Ramadhaan'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ شَقِيقٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ، عَنْ صِيَامِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ يَصُومُ حَتَّى نَقُولَ قَدْ صَامَ، وَيُفْطِرُ حَتَّى نَقُولَ قَدْ أَفْطَرَ قَالَتْ: وَمَا صَامَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، شَهْرًا كَامِلا مُنْذُ قَدِمَ الْمَدِينَةَ إِلا رَمَضَانَ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 281

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 282
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Someone was asked about the saum of Messenger of Allah ﷺ? He replied: 'It was his noble habit to fast on different occasions. In some months he fasted for so many days, that it was thought he would continue fasting. In other months he did not fast, we thought he would not fast now. It was also from his noble habits that if one wanted to observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed salaah at night, it was possible, and if one wanted to observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ sleep at night, this too was possible'".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Someone was asked about the saum of Messenger of Allah ﷺ? He replied: 'It was his noble habit to fast on different occasions. In some months he fasted for so many days, that it was thought he would continue fasting. In other months he did not fast, we thought he would not fast now. It was also from his noble habits that if one wanted to observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed salaah at night, it was possible, and if one wanted to observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ sleep at night, this too was possible'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنْ صَوْمِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَقَالَ: كَانَ يَصُومُ مِنَ الشَّهْرِ حَتَّى نَرَى أَنْ لا يُرِيدَ أَنْ يُفْطِرَ مِنْهُ، وَيُفْطِرُ مِنْهُ حَتَّى نَرَى أَنْ لا يُرِيدَ أَنْ يَصُومَ مِنْهُ شَيْئًا وَكُنْتَ لا تَشَاءُ أَنْ تَرَاهُ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ مُصَلِّيًا إِلا رَأَيْتَهُ مُصَلِّيًا، وَلا نَائِمًا إِلا رَأَيْتَهُ نَائِمًا.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 282

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 283
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted the major portion of the month at times, till we thought that he did not intend ending the fasts. In some months he did not fast, we began to think he would not fast now. Besides Ramadaan he did not fast for a full month".
narrator: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted the major portion of the month at times, till we thought that he did not intend ending the fasts. In some months he did not fast, we began to think he would not fast now. Besides Ramadaan he did not fast for a full month".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي بِشْرٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَصُومُ حَتَّى نَقُولَ مَا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُفْطِرَ مِنْهُ، وَيُفْطِرُ حَتَّى نَقُولَ مَا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَصُومَ مِنْهُ، وَمَا صَامَ شَهْرًا كَامِلا مُنْذُ قَدِمَ الْمَدِينَةَ إِلا رَمَضَانَ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 283

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 284
englishRaw:
Ummi Salaah (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "I did not observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ fast for two consecutive months, besides the month of Sha'baan and Ramadhaan".
narrator: Ummi Salaah (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "I did not observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ fast for two consecutive months, besides the month of Sha'baan and Ramadhaan".
arabic:
حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ سَالِمِ بْنِ أَبِي الْجَعْدِ، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَتْ: مَا رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ يَصُومُ شَهْرَيْنِ مُتَتَابِعَيْنِ إِلا شَعْبَانَ وَرَمَضَانَ 

قَالَ أَبُو عِيسَى: هَذَا إِسنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ وَهَكَذَا، قَالَ: عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ وَرَوَى هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، وَيُحْتَمَلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ أَبُو سَلَمَةَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ قَدْ رَوَى الْحَدِيثَ عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، وَأُمِّ سَلَمَةَ جَمِيعًا، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 284

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 285
englishRaw:
"Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
                      "I did not observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ fast for more days in any month (excluding Ramadaan) other than Sha'baan. He fasted for the major part of the month, and nearly fasted for the full month"
narrator: "Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
body: "I did not observe Messenger of Allah ﷺ fast for more days in any month (excluding Ramadaan) other than Sha'baan. He fasted for the major part of the month, and nearly fasted for the full month"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَنَّادٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: لَمْ أَرَ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، يَصُومُ فِي شَهْرٍ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ صِيَامِهِ لِلَّهِ فِي شَعْبَانَ، كَانَ يَصُومُ شَعْبَانَ إِلا قَلِيلا، بَلْ كَانَ يَصُومُهُ كُلَّهُ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 285

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 286
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted for three days at the beginning of every month, and he very seldom did not fast on Friday".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted for three days at the beginning of every month, and he very seldom did not fast on Friday".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ دِينَارٍ الْكُوفِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللهِ بْنُ مُوسَى، وَطَلْقُ بْنُ غَنَّامٍ، عَنْ شَيْبَانَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ زِرِّ بْنُ حُبَيْشٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يَصُومُ مِنْ غُرَّةِ كُلِّ شَهْرٍ ثَلاثَةَ أَيَامٍ، وَقَلَّمَا كَانَ يُفْطِرُ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 286

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 287
englishRaw:
Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ (often) gave importance to the fasting on Mondays and Thursdays"
narrator: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ (often) gave importance to the fasting on Mondays and Thursdays"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو حَفْصٍ عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ دَاوُدَ، عَنْ ثَوْرِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ مَعْدَانَ، عَنْ رَبِيعَةَ الْجُرَشِيِّ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَتَحَرَّى صَوْمَ الاثْنَيْنِ وَالْخَمِيسِ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 287

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 288
englishRaw:
Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Deeds are presented (before Allah subhanahu wata'allah) on Mondays and Thursdays. I desire that my deeds be presented whilst I am fasting'".
narrator: Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Deeds are presented (before Allah subhanahu wata'allah) on Mondays and Thursdays. I desire that my deeds be presented whilst I am fasting'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ رِفَاعَةَ، عَنْ سُهَيْلِ بْنِ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، قَالَ: تُعْرَضُ الأَعْمَالُ يَوْمَ الاثْنَيْنِ وَالْخَمِيسِ، فَأُحِبُّ أَنْ يُعْرَضَ عَمَلِي وَأَنَا صَائِمٌ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 288

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 289
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted three days of every month. In some months he fasted on Saturdays, Sundays and Mondays, and in some months he fasted on Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ fasted three days of every month. In some months he fasted on Saturdays, Sundays and Mondays, and in some months he fasted on Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أَحْمَدَ، وَمُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، قَالا: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يَصُومُ مِنَ الشَّهْرِ السَّبْتَ وَالأَحَدَ وَالاثْنَيْنَ، وَمِنَ الشَّهْرِ الآخَرِ الثُّلاثَاءَ وَالأَرْبَعَاءَ وَالْخَمِيسَ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 289

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 290
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not fast in any month more than in the month of Sha'baan".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not fast in any month more than in the month of Sha'baan".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُصْعَبٍ الْمَدِينِيُّ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ أَبِي النَّضْرِ، عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: مَا كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يَصُومُ فِي شَهْرٍ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ صِيَامِهِ فِي شَعْبَانَ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 290

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 291
englishRaw:
Mu'aadhah (may Allah be pleased with her) says. "I inquired from 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), did Messenger of Allah ﷺ fast for three days of every month?" She replied:
                      "Yes". I then asked: "On which days of the month did he fast". She replied. "He did not fast on specific days, but whenever suitable".
narrator: Mu'aadhah (may Allah be pleased with her) says. "I inquired from 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), did Messenger of Allah ﷺ fast for three days of every month?" She replied:
body: "Yes". I then asked: "On which days of the month did he fast". She replied. "He did not fast on specific days, but whenever suitable".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ الرِّشْكِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ مُعَاذَةَ، قَالَتْ: قُلْتُ لِعَائِشَةَ: أَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يَصُومُ ثَلاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ مِنْ كُلِّ شَهْرٍ؟ قَالَتْ: نَعَمْ قُلْتُ: مِنْ أَيِّهِ كَانَ يَصُومُ؟ قَالَتْ: كَانَ لا يُبَالِي مِنْ أَيِّهِ صَامَ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 291

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 292
englishRaw:
Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "The Quraysh observed the fast of 'Aa-shura in the days of jaahiliyyah (pre-Islaarnic period of ignorance). Messenger of Allah ﷺ also observed this fast (before the hijrah as a nafl). (After the hijrah) when he came to Madinah Munawwarah he observed these and commanded the ummah also to observe it. When the command to fast in Ramadaan was revealed, it was proclaimed fard, and the fast of 'Aa-shura became nafl. Those who wished, observed them ('Aa-shura) and those who did not, omitted them.
narrator: Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "The Quraysh observed the fast of 'Aa-shura in the days of jaahiliyyah (pre-Islaarnic period of ignorance). Messenger of Allah ﷺ also observed this fast (before the hijrah as a nafl). (After the hijrah) when he came to Madinah Munawwarah he observed these and commanded the ummah also to observe it. When the command to fast in Ramadaan was revealed, it was proclaimed fard, and the fast of 'Aa-shura became nafl. Those who wished, observed them ('Aa-shura) and those who did not, omitted them.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ الْهَمْدَانِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ عَاشُورَاءُ يَوْمًا تَصُومُهُ قُرَيْشٌ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، وَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يَصُومُهُ، فَلَمَّا قَدِمَ الْمَدِينَةَ صَامَهُ وَأَمَرَ بِصِيَامِهِ، فَلَمَّا افْتُرِضَ رَمَضَانُ كَانَ رَمَضَانُ هُوَ الْفَرِيضَةُ وَتُرِكَ عَاشُورَاءُ، فَمَنْ شَاءَ صَامَهُ وَمَنْ شَاءَ تَرَكَهُ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 292

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 293
englishRaw:
'Alqamah (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
                      "I asked Aisha Radhiyallahu anha: 'Did Messenger of Allah ﷺ fix a day for 'Ibaadah? She replied: 'The practices of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was of a continuous nature. Who among you have the strength, which Messenger of Allah ﷺ had?'"
narrator: 'Alqamah (may Allah be pleased with him) relates:
body: "I asked Aisha Radhiyallahu anha: 'Did Messenger of Allah ﷺ fix a day for 'Ibaadah? She replied: 'The practices of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was of a continuous nature. Who among you have the strength, which Messenger of Allah ﷺ had?'"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ، أَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يَخُصُّ مِنَ الأَيَامِ شَيْئًا؟ قَالَتْ: كَانَ عَمَلُهُ دِيمَةً، وَأَيُّكُمْ يُطِيقُ مَا كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يُطِيقُ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 293

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 294
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once came home, a woman was present at that time. Messenger of Allah ﷺ inquired. 'Who is this woman?' I replied: 'This is a certain woman who does not sleep at night'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'One should observe only that amount of nawaafil which one can bear. I swear an oath that Allah Ta'aala does not fear the granting of rewards, till you begin to fear the observance of deeds'. 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). 'Anha says: 'The most beloved deed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were those that were practised continuously.
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once came home, a woman was present at that time. Messenger of Allah ﷺ inquired. 'Who is this woman?' I replied: 'This is a certain woman who does not sleep at night'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'One should observe only that amount of nawaafil which one can bear. I swear an oath that Allah Ta'aala does not fear the granting of rewards, till you begin to fear the observance of deeds'. 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). 'Anha says: 'The most beloved deed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ were those that were practised continuously.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: دَخَلَ عَلَيَّ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ وَعِنْدِي امْرَأَةٌ، فَقَالَ: مَنْ هَذِهِ؟ قُلْتُ: فُلانَةُ لا تَنَامُ اللَّيْلَ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ: عَلَيْكُمْ مِنَ الأَعْمَالِ مَا تُطِيقُونَ، فَوَاللَّهِ لا يَمَلُّ اللَّهُ حَتَّى تَمَلُّوا، وَكَانَ أَحَبَّ ذَلِكَ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ الَّذِي يَدُومُ عَلَيْهِ صَاحِبُهُ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 294

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 295
englishRaw: Abu Salih (may Allah be pleased with him) reports "I enquired from Aisha and Ummi Salamah (may Allah be pleased with him)ma that which act was the most beloved by Messenger of Allah ﷺ?" Both gave the reply "That deed which was practised continuously, even if it was a little".
body: Abu Salih (may Allah be pleased with him) reports "I enquired from Aisha and Ummi Salamah (may Allah be pleased with him)ma that which act was the most beloved by Messenger of Allah ﷺ?" Both gave the reply "That deed which was practised continuously, even if it was a little".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو هِشَامٍ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ الرِّفَاعِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ، وَأُمَّ سَلَمَةَ، أَيُّ الْعَمَلِ كَانَ أَحَبَّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ؟ قَالَتَا: مَا دِيمَ عَلَيْهِ، وَإِنْ قَلَّ.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 295

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 296
englishRaw:
Awf bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I spent a night with Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ used the miswaak (cleaned the teeth), then performed the wudhu, then stood up in salaah. I stood with him (joined him). He began reciting the Surah Baqarah. Whenever he came across an aayah of mercy, he paused and beseeched Allah of His Mercy. In the same manner when he came across an aayah of adhaab (punishment), he paused and beseeched Allah for His forgiveness from adhaab. He then performed ruku', and remained in the ruku for as long as he had spent in the standing posture. He recited in the ruku':Subhaana dhil jabaruti wal-malakuti wal-kibri-yaa-i wal-'a-za-mati.Translation: Glory be to the Lord of the Might, the Dominion, the Majesty, and the Magnificence.Thereafter he performed the sajdah, which was as long as the ruku and recited the same du'aa in the sajdah. He then recited the Surah Aali Imraan (in the second rakah), thereafter one surah (in each rakah) and did the same"
narrator: Awf bin Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I spent a night with Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ used the miswaak (cleaned the teeth), then performed the wudhu, then stood up in salaah. I stood with him (joined him). He began reciting the Surah Baqarah. Whenever he came across an aayah of mercy, he paused and beseeched Allah of His Mercy. In the same manner when he came across an aayah of adhaab (punishment), he paused and beseeched Allah for His forgiveness from adhaab. He then performed ruku', and remained in the ruku for as long as he had spent in the standing posture. He recited in the ruku':Subhaana dhil jabaruti wal-malakuti wal-kibri-yaa-i wal-'a-za-mati.Translation: Glory be to the Lord of the Might, the Dominion, the Majesty, and the Magnificence.Thereafter he performed the sajdah, which was as long as the ruku and recited the same du'aa in the sajdah. He then recited the Surah Aali Imraan (in the second rakah), thereafter one surah (in each rakah) and did the same"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ قَيْسٍ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ عَاصِمَ بْنَ حُمَيْدٍ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَوْفَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ، يَقُولُ: كُنْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، لَيْلَةً فَاسْتَاكَ، ثُمَّ تَوَضَّأَ، ثُمَّ قَامَ يُصَلِّي، فَقُمْتُ مَعَهُ فَبَدَأَ فَاسْتَفْتَحَ الْبَقَرَةَ، فَلا يَمُرُّ بِآيَةِ رَحْمَةٍ، إِلا وَقَفَ فَسَأَلَ، وَلا يَمُرُّ بِآيَةِ عَذَابٍ، إِلا وَقَفَ فَتَعَوَّذَ، ثُمَّ رَكَعَ فَمَكَثَ رَاكِعًا بِقَدْرِ قِيَامِهِ، وَيَقُولُ فِي رُكُوعِهِ: سُبْحَانَ ذِي الْجَبَرُوتِ وَالْمَلَكُوتِ، وَالْكِبْرِيَاءِ وَالْعَظَمَةِ، ثُمَّ سَجَدَ بِقَدْرِ رُكُوعِهِ، وَيَقُولُ فِي سُجُودِهِ: سُبْحَانَ ذِي الْجَبَرُوتِ وَالْمَلَكُوتِ، وَالْكِبْرِيَاءِ وَالْعَظَمَةِ ثُمَّ قَرَأَ آلَ عِمْرَانَ ثُمَّ سُورَةً، يَفْعَلُ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ في كل ركعة.
references: Book of The Fasting Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 296

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 297
englishRaw:
Ya'laa bin Mamlak (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "He asked Ummul Mu'mineen Sayyiditina Ummi Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) about the recital of Messenger of Allah ﷺ". She replied: "He recited every word separately and clearly".
narrator: Ya'laa bin Mamlak (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "He asked Ummul Mu'mineen Sayyiditina Ummi Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) about the recital of Messenger of Allah ﷺ". She replied: "He recited every word separately and clearly".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا اللَّيْثُ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ، عَنْ يَعَلَى بْنِ مَمْلَكٍ، أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قِرَاءَةِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، فَإِذَا هِيَ تَنْعَتُ قِرَاءَةً مُفَسَّرَةً حَرْفًا حَرْفًا.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 297

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 298
englishRaw:
Qataadah (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I inquired from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him): 'How was the recital of Messenger of Allah ﷺ ?' He replied: 'He recited (The words of madd) with a madd'".
narrator: Qataadah (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I inquired from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him): 'How was the recital of Messenger of Allah ﷺ ?' He replied: 'He recited (The words of madd) with a madd'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَهْبُ بْنُ جَرِيرِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لأَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ: كَيْفَ كَانَتْ قِرَاءَةُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ؟ فَقَالَ: مَدًّا.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 298

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 299
englishRaw:
Ummi Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited every aayah separately. He recited 'Alhamdulillaahi Rabbil 'Aa-lameen' and paused, then recited 'Ar Rahmaanir Raheem' and paused. Then paused after reciting 'Maaliki Yawmiddeen'"
narrator: Ummi Salamah (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited every aayah separately. He recited 'Alhamdulillaahi Rabbil 'Aa-lameen' and paused, then recited 'Ar Rahmaanir Raheem' and paused. Then paused after reciting 'Maaliki Yawmiddeen'"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ الأُمَوِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، قَالَتْ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ، يَقْطَعُ قِرَاءَتَهُ، يَقُولُ: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ ثُمَّ يَقِفُ، ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ ثُمَّ يَقِفُ، وَكَانَ يَقْرَأُ مَلِكِ يَوْمِ الدِّينِ.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 299

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 300
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Abi Qays (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "I inquired from 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) about the recital of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, did he 'recite softly or audibly? She replied: 'He recited in both ways'. I said: 'Alhamdulillaah, (Praise be to, Allah), who has accorded us easiness in the matter"'. (According to the situation whatever was proper, reciting softly or audibly was adopted)
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Abi Qays (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "I inquired from 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) about the recital of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, did he 'recite softly or audibly? She replied: 'He recited in both ways'. I said: 'Alhamdulillaah, (Praise be to, Allah), who has accorded us easiness in the matter"'. (According to the situation whatever was proper, reciting softly or audibly was adopted)
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا اللَّيْثُ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ أَبِي قَيْسٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ، عَنْ قِرَاءَةِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَكَانَ يُسِرُّ بِالْقِرَاءَةِ أَمْ يَجْهَرُ؟ قَالَتْ: كُلُّ ذَلِكَ قَدْ كَانَ يَفْعَلُ قَدْ كَانَ رُبَّمَا أَسَرَّ وَرُبَّمَا جَهَرَ فَقُلْتُ: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الَّذِي جَعَلَ فِي الأَمْرِ سَعَةً.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 300

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 301
englishRaw:
Ummi Haani (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "I heard Messenger of Allah ﷺ reciting (the Qur-aan in the Masjidul Haraam) at night, while I was on the rooftop of my house".
narrator: Ummi Haani (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "I heard Messenger of Allah ﷺ reciting (the Qur-aan in the Masjidul Haraam) at night, while I was on the rooftop of my house".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مِسْعَرٌ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَلاءِ الْعَبْدِيِّ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ جَعْدَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّ هَانِئٍ، قَالَتْ: كُنْتُ أَسْمَعُ قِرَاءَةَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، بِاللَّيْلِ وَأَنَا عَلَى عَرِيشِي.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 301

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 302
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ riding his camel on the day when Makkah was conquered, he was reciting:"Lo! We have given thee (O Muhammad) a signal victory, That Allah may forgive thee of thy sin that which is past and that which is to come..." - Surah Fath: 1-2.He says: "He read it and repeated it". Mu'aawiyah bin Qurrah (may Allah be pleased with him) (who is a narrator in the chain of this hadith) says: ''If I did not fear the people would surround me, I should have recited it in the same tone'"
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Mughaffal (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I saw Messenger of Allah ﷺ riding his camel on the day when Makkah was conquered, he was reciting:"Lo! We have given thee (O Muhammad) a signal victory, That Allah may forgive thee of thy sin that which is past and that which is to come..." - Surah Fath: 1-2.He says: "He read it and repeated it". Mu'aawiyah bin Qurrah (may Allah be pleased with him) (who is a narrator in the chain of this hadith) says: ''If I did not fear the people would surround me, I should have recited it in the same tone'"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ قُرَّةَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَبْدَ اللهِ بْنَ مُغَفَّلٍ، يَقُولُ: رَأَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، عَلَى نَاقَتِهِ يَوْمَ الْفَتْحِ، وَهُوَ يَقْرَأُ: إِنَّا فَتَحْنَا لَكَ فَتْحًا مُبِينًا لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ، قَالَ: فَقَرَأَ وَرَجَّعَ، قَالَ: وَقَالَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ قُرَّةَ: لَوْلا أَنْ يَجْتَمِعَ النَّاسُ عَلَيَّ لأَخَذْتُ لَكُمْ فِي ذَلِكَ الصَّوْتِ أَوْ قَالَ: اللَّحْنِ.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 302

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 303
englishRaw: Qataadah (may Allah be pleased with him) narrates that Allah gave to every Nabi that He had sent a beautiful feature and beautiful voice. Your Nabi ﷺ also had a beautiful feature and a beautiful voice. Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam did not recite in a melodious tone as singers do.
body: Qataadah (may Allah be pleased with him) narrates that Allah gave to every Nabi that He had sent a beautiful feature and beautiful voice. Your Nabi ﷺ also had a beautiful feature and a beautiful voice. Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam did not recite in a melodious tone as singers do.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا نُوحُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ الْحُدَّانِيُّ، عَنْ حُسَامِ بْنِ مِصَكٍّ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: مَا بَعَثَ اللَّهُ نَبِيًّا إِلا حَسَنَ الْوَجْهِ، حَسَنَ الصَّوْتِ، وَكَانَ نَبِيُّكُمْ ﷺ حَسَنَ الْوَجْهِ، حَسَنَ الصَّوْتِ، وَكَانَ لا يُرَجِّعُ.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 303

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 304
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ raised his voice only to the extent, that it might have been possible that if he recited in the house, those in the courtyard might be able to listen".
narrator: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ raised his voice only to the extent, that it might have been possible that if he recited in the house, those in the courtyard might be able to listen".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ حَسَّانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي الزِّنَادِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ أَبِي عَمْرٍو، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: كَانَتْ قِرَاءَةُ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، رُبَّمَا يَسْمَعُهَا مَنْ فِي الْحُجْرَةِ وَهُوَ فِي الْبَيْتِ.
references: Book of The Recital Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 304

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 305
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Shikh-kheer (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I attended the noble assembly of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He was performing salaah. Because of his crying, such sound emmitted from his chest, like that of a boiling pot".
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Shikh-kheer (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I attended the noble assembly of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He was performing salaah. Because of his crying, such sound emmitted from his chest, like that of a boiling pot".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُوَيْدُ بْنُ نَصْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ الْمُبَارِكِ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ وَهُوَ ابْنُ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ الشِّخِّيرِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ وَهُوَ يُصَلِّي، وَلِجَوْفِهِ أَزِيزٌ كَأَزِيزِ الْمِرْجَلِ مِنَ الْبُكَاءِ.
references: Book of The Weeping Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 305

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 306
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once asked me to recite the Qur-aan to him. (He might have said this because one might be able to concentrate more when one is listens, or he may have said this for another reason, which may include many reasons. For example, the hearing of the recital of the Qur-aan is regarded as a sunnah, as a result of this deed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ) I said: ''O Messenger of Allah, should I recite it to you when it has been revealed to you?' (Sayyidina ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) may have thought this recital was for tabligh as a reminder) Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I love to hear it from another person'' Thereupon I began reciting Surah Nisaa (which begins from the last quarter of the 4th juz). When I reached this aayah: ''But how (will it be with them) when We bring of every people a witness, and We bring thee (O Muhammed) a witness against these?''-Surah Nisaa,41 I saw tears flowing from both eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
narrator: 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once asked me to recite the Qur-aan to him. (He might have said this because one might be able to concentrate more when one is listens, or he may have said this for another reason, which may include many reasons. For example, the hearing of the recital of the Qur-aan is regarded as a sunnah, as a result of this deed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ) I said: ''O Messenger of Allah, should I recite it to you when it has been revealed to you?'
