(Sayyidina ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) may have thought this recital was for tabligh as a reminder) Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I love to hear it from another person'' Thereupon I began reciting Surah Nisaa (which begins from the last quarter of the 4th juz). When I reached this aayah: ''But how (will it be with them) when We bring of every people a witness, and We bring thee (O Muhammed) a witness against these?''-Surah Nisaa,41 I saw tears flowing from both eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ الأَعْمَشِ، عَنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ: اقْرَأْ عَلَيَّ فَقُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، أَقَرَأُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَيْكَ أُنْزِلَ، قَالَ: إِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَسْمَعَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِي، فَقَرَأْتُ سُورَةَ النِّسَاءِ، حَتَّى بَلَغْتُ وَجِئِنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلاءِ شَهِيدًا، قَالَ: فَرَأَيْتُ عَيْنَيْ رَسُولِ اللهِ تَهْمِلانِ.
references: Book of The Weeping Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 306

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 307
englishRaw:
'Abdullah bin 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "In the time of Messenger of Allah ﷺ there once occurred a solar eclipse (According to the majority of the 'ulama this incident took place in the tenth year hijri). Messenger of Allah ﷺ went into the masjid, commenced salaah, and stood in qiyaam for so long that it was felt that he did not intend to perform the ruku'. (In another narration it is stated that he recited the Surah Baqarah.) He then performed such a long ruku as if he did not want to come up from the Ruku'. Then in the same manner after standing up from the ruku' he stood up for such a long time as if he did not want to perform sajdah, here too he kept his mubaarak head on the ground for such a long time as if he was not going to lift his mubaarak head. In this manner he did the same after lifting the head and sitting in jalsa, and after the jalsa in the second sajdah.In short, in every rukn of the salaah this was done, that every rukn was so long, as if this rukn was going to be performed till the end, and there is nothing after it. (In the same manner he performed the second rak'ah, and in the last sajdah), due to the intense fear he began taking heavy breaths and crying, and began pleading to the Almighty Allah that 'O Allah, it is only You that have promised that when these people make istighfaar there will be no punishment'. This saying of Messenger of Allah ﷺ refers to the aayah that is at the end of the ninth juz: ''But Allah would not punish them while thou was with them, nor will He punish them while they seek forgiveness. - Surah Al-Anfaal, 33.When Messenger of Allah ﷺ completed the salaah, the sun had cleared already. Messenger of Allah Sallahu 'Alayhi Wasallam delivered a sermon after this. After uttering the hamd and thanaa, he talked o this subject, that the sun and moon does not eclipse because of the death or birth of anyone, but both are from among the signs of Allah Ta'aala. (That gives His creation a warning so that they may fear Him). When the eclipses occurs then immediately turn towards Allah (begin istighfaar and performing salaah)"
narrator: 'Abdullah bin 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "In the time of Messenger of Allah ﷺ there once occurred a solar eclipse (According to the majority of the 'ulama this incident took place in the tenth year hijri). Messenger of Allah ﷺ went into the masjid, commenced salaah, and stood in qiyaam for so long that it was felt that he did not intend to perform the ruku'. (In another narration it is stated that he recited the Surah Baqarah.) He then performed such a long ruku as if he did not want to come up from the Ruku'. Then in the same manner after standing up from the ruku' he stood up for such a long time as if he did not want to perform sajdah, here too he kept his mubaarak head on the ground for such a long time as if he was not going to lift his mubaarak head. In this manner he did the same after lifting the head and sitting in jalsa, and after the jalsa in the second sajdah.In short, in every rukn of the salaah this was done, that every rukn was so long, as if this rukn was going to be performed till the end, and there is nothing after it. (In the same manner he performed the second rak'ah, and in the last sajdah), due to the intense fear he began taking heavy breaths and crying, and began pleading to the Almighty Allah that 'O Allah, it is only You that have promised that when these people make istighfaar there will be no punishment'. This saying of Messenger of Allah ﷺ refers to the aayah that is at the end of the ninth juz: ''But Allah would not punish them while thou was with them, nor will He punish them while they seek forgiveness. - Surah Al-Anfaal, 33.When Messenger of Allah ﷺ completed the salaah, the sun had cleared already. Messenger of Allah Sallahu 'Alayhi Wasallam delivered a sermon after this. After uttering the hamd and thanaa, he talked o this subject, that the sun and moon does not eclipse because of the death or birth of anyone, but both are from among the signs of Allah Ta'aala. (That gives His creation a warning so that they may fear Him). When the eclipses occurs then immediately turn towards Allah (begin istighfaar and performing salaah)"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، قَالَ: انْكسفَتِ الشَّمْسُ يَوْمًا عَلَى عَهْدِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، فَقَامَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يُصَلِّي، حَتَّى لَمْ يَكَدْ يَرْكَعُ ثُمَّ رَكَعَ، فَلَمْ يَكَدْ يَرْفَعُ رَأْسَهُ، ثُمَّ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ، فَلَمْ يَكَدْ أَنْ يَسْجُدَ، ثُمَّ سَجَدَ فَلَمْ يَكَدْ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ رَأْسَهُ، ثُمَّ رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ، فَلَمْ يَكَدْ أَنْ يَسْجُدَ، ثُمَّ سَجَدَ فَلَمْ يَكَدْ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ رَأْسَهُ، فَجَعَلَ يَنْفُخُ وَيَبْكِي، وَيَقُولُ: رَبِّ أَلَمْ تَعِدْنِي أَنْ لا تُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَأَنَا فِيهِمْ؟ رَبِّ أَلَمْ تَعِدْنِي أَنْ لا تُعَذِّبَهُمْ وَهُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ؟ وَنَحْنُ نَسْتَغْفِرُكَ فَلَمَّا صَلَّى رَكْعَتَيْنِ انْجَلَتِ الشَّمْسُ، فَقَامَ فَحَمِدَ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى، وَأَثْنَى عَلَيْهِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: إِنَّ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ آيَتَانِ مِنْ آيَاتِ اللهِ لا يَنْكَسِفَانِ لِمَوْتِ أَحَدٍ وَلا لِحَيَاتِهِ، فَإِذَا انْكَسَفَا، فَافْزَعُوا إِلَى ذِكْرِ اللهِ تَعَالَى.
references: Book of The Weeping Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 307

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 308
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that one of the daughters of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was on her death bed. Messenger of Allah ﷺ picked her up and put her before him. She passed away in his presence. Ummi Ayman (who was a slave girl of Messenger of Allah ﷺ) began wailing aloud. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
                      "Are you crying before the Messenger of Allah?" (because tears were also flowing from the eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ). She said: 'Do I not see you cry?' Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: "This crying is not prohibited. It is a mercy of Allah". (He softens the hearts of His servants, and creates love and mercy in them). Messenger of Allah ﷺ then said: "A Muslim is at peace at all times. even when his soul is being taken out, he is busy uttering the hamd (praises) of Allah".
narrator: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that one of the daughters of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was on her death bed. Messenger of Allah ﷺ picked her up and put her before him. She passed away in his presence. Ummi Ayman (who was a slave girl of Messenger of Allah ﷺ) began wailing aloud. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
body: "Are you crying before the Messenger of Allah?" (because tears were also flowing from the eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ). She said: 'Do I not see you cry?' Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: "This crying is not prohibited. It is a mercy of Allah". (He softens the hearts of His servants, and creates love and mercy in them). Messenger of Allah ﷺ then said: "A Muslim is at peace at all times. even when his soul is being taken out, he is busy uttering the hamd (praises) of Allah".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أَحْمَدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: أَخَذَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ ابْنَةً لَهُ تَقْضِي فَاحْتَضَنَهَا فَوَضَعَهَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ، فَمَاتَتْ وَهِيَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَصَاحَتْ أُمُّ أَيْمَنَ، فَقَالَ يَعْنِي ﷺ: أَتَبْكِينَ عِنْدَ رَسُولِ اللهِ؟ فَقَالَتْ: أَلَسْتُ أَرَاكَ تَبْكِي؟ قَالَ: إِنِّي لَسْتُ أَبْكِي، إِنَّمَا هِيَ رَحْمَةٌ، إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ بِكُلِّ خَيْرٍ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ، إِنَّ نَفْسَهُ تُنْزَعُ مِنْ بَيْنِ جَنْبَيْهِ، وَهُوَ يَحْمَدُ اللَّهَ تعالى .
references: Book of The Weeping Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 308

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 309
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ kissed the forehead of 'Uthmaan bin Maz'oon after his death. At that time tears were flowing from his eyes".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ kissed the forehead of 'Uthmaan bin Maz'oon after his death. At that time tears were flowing from his eyes".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللهِ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، قَبَّلَ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ مَظْعُونٍ وَهُوَ مَيِّتٌ وَهُوَ يَبْكِي أَوْ قَالَ: عَيْنَاهُ تَهْرَاقَانِ.
references: Book of The Weeping Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 309

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 310
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was sitting at the grave of his daughter (Sayyiditina Ummi Kulthum (may Allah be pleased with her)) and tears were flowing from his eyes. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'That person should enter the grave who did not have sexual relations that (previous) night'. Abu Talhah (may Allah be pleased with him) replied: 'I did not'. At the request of Messenger of Allah ﷺ he entered her grave"
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was sitting at the grave of his daughter (Sayyiditina Ummi Kulthum (may Allah be pleased with her)) and tears were flowing from his eyes. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'That person should enter the grave who did not have sexual relations that (previous) night'. Abu Talhah (may Allah be pleased with him) replied: 'I did not'. At the request of Messenger of Allah ﷺ he entered her grave"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا فُلَيْحٌ وَهُوَ ابْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ هِلالِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: شَهِدْنَا ابْنَةً لِرَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، وَرَسُولُ اللهِ جَالِسٌ عَلَى الْقَبْرِ، فَرَأَيْتُ عَيْيَنْهِ تَدمَعَانِ، فَقَالَ: أَفِيكُمْ رَجُلٌ لَمْ يُقَارِفِ اللَّيْلَةَ؟، قَالَ أَبُو طَلْحَةَ: أَنَا، قَالَ: انْزِلْ فَنَزَلَ فِي قَبْرِهَا.
references: Book of The Weeping Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 310

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 311
englishRaw: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that the bed on which Messenger of Allah ﷺ slept was made of leather, in which was filled coir of the palm tree.
body: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that the bed on which Messenger of Allah ﷺ slept was made of leather, in which was filled coir of the palm tree.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَ: إِنَّمَا كَانَ فِرَاشُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، الَّذِي يَنَامُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَدَمٍ، حَشْوُهُ لِيفٌ.
references: Book of Narrations Of The Bed Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 311

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 312
englishRaw:
Imaam Muhammad AI-Baaqir (may Allah be pleased with him) says that someone asked Ummul Mu'mineen 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her):
                      'How was the bed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in your house?' She replied: 'It was made of leather, in which was filled the coir of the date palm'. Hafsah (may Allah be pleased with her) was asked how was the bed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in your house? She replied: 'It was a canvas folded into two, which was spread for Messenger of Allah ﷺ to sleep on. On one night I thought if I folded it into four and spread it, it would become softer. I folded it and spread it that way. In the morning Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked: 'What did you spread for me last night? I replied: 'It was the same bed, I only folded it into four so that it may become softer'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Leave it in its original form. Its softness deprived me of my prayers (tahajjud) at night'.
narrator: Imaam Muhammad AI-Baaqir (may Allah be pleased with him) says that someone asked Ummul Mu'mineen 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her):
body: 'How was the bed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in your house?' She replied: 'It was made of leather, in which was filled the coir of the date palm'. Hafsah (may Allah be pleased with her) was asked how was the bed of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in your house? She replied: 'It was a canvas folded into two, which was spread for Messenger of Allah ﷺ to sleep on. On one night I thought if I folded it into four and spread it, it would become softer. I folded it and spread it that way. In the morning Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked: 'What did you spread for me last night? I replied: 'It was the same bed, I only folded it into four so that it may become softer'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Leave it in its original form. Its softness deprived me of my prayers (tahajjud) at night'.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْخَطَّابِ زِيَادُ بْنُ يَحْيَى الْبَصْرِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ مَيْمُونٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: سُئِلَتْ عَائِشَةُ،: -. وَسُئِلَتْ حَفْصَةُ، مَا كَانَ فِرَاشُ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ فِي بَيْتِكِ؟ قَالَتْ: مِسْحًا نَثْنِيهِ ثَنِيَّتَيْنِ فَيَنَامُ عَلَيْهِ، فَلَمَّا كَانَ ذَاتَ لَيْلَةٍ، قُلْتُ: لَوْ ثَنَيْتَهُ أَرْبَعَ ثَنْيَاتٍ، لَكَانَ أَوْطَأَ لَهُ، فَثَنَيْنَاهُ لَهُ بِأَرْبَعِ ثَنْيَاتٍ، فَلَمَّا أَصْبَحَ، قَالَ: مَا فَرشْتُمْ لِيَ اللَّيْلَةَ قَالَتْ: قُلْنَا: هُوَ فِرَاشُكَ، إِلا أَنَّا ثَنَيْنَاهُ بِأَرْبَعِ ثَنْيَاتٍ، قُلْنَا: هُوَ أَوْطَأُ لَكَ، قَالَ: رُدُّوهُ لِحَالَتِهِ الأُولَى، فَإِنَّهُ مَنَعَتْنِي وَطَاءَتُهُ صَلاتيَ اللَّيْلَةَ.
references: Book of Narrations Of The Bed Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 312

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 313
englishRaw:
'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      ''Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not exceed in praising me as the Christians over-praised 'Esa 'Alayhis Salaam. (That they made him the son of God.) I am a bondsman of Allah, therefore, call me the bondsman of Allah and His Rasul'".
narrator: 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: ''Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not exceed in praising me as the Christians over-praised 'Esa 'Alayhis Salaam. (That they made him the son of God.) I am a bondsman of Allah, therefore, call me the bondsman of Allah and His Rasul'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنِيعٍ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْمَخْزُومِيُّ، وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، قَالُوا: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ: لا تُطْرُونِي كَمَا أَطْرَتِ النَّصَارَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ، إِنَّمَا أَنَا عَبْدٌ، فَقُولُوا: عَبْدُ اللهِ وَرَسُولُهُ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 313

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 314
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      A woman came to Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: I would like to speak to you in private'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: 'Sit on any street of Madinah and I will come there and listen to you'".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: A woman came to Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: I would like to speak to you in private'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: 'Sit on any street of Madinah and I will come there and listen to you'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُوَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، أَنَّ امْرَأَةً جَاءَتْ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَقَالَتْ لَهُ: إِنَّ لِي إِلَيْكَ حَاجَةً، فَقَالَ: اجْلِسِي فِي أَيِّ طَرِيقِ الْمَدِينَةِ شِئْتِ، أَجْلِسْ إِلَيْكِ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 314

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 315
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ visited the sick, attended funerals, rode on donkeys, accepted the invitations of slaves. On the day of the battle of Banu Qurayzah, he rode on a donkey, the reigns of which were made of date palm leaves. On it was also a saddle made of date palm leaves".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ visited the sick, attended funerals, rode on donkeys, accepted the invitations of slaves. On the day of the battle of Banu Qurayzah, he rode on a donkey, the reigns of which were made of date palm leaves. On it was also a saddle made of date palm leaves".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ مُسْلِمٍ الأَعْوَرِ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يَعُودُ الْمَرِيضَ، وَيَشْهَدُ الْجَنَائِزَ، وَيَرْكَبُ الْحِمَارَ، وَيُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الْعَبْدِ، وَكَانَ يَوْمَ بَنِي قُرَيْظَةَ عَلَى حِمَارٍ مَخْطُومٍ بَحَبْلٍ مِنْ لِيفٍ، وَعَلَيْهِ إِكَافٌ مِنْ لِيفٍ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 315

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 316
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      ''Messenger of Allah ﷺ accepted and attended invitations where bread made of barley, and stale fat a few days old was served (Without hesitation he accepted these invitations). Messenger of Allah ﷺ had pawned his armour to a Jew. Till the end of his life Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not possess a sufficient amount to release that armour'".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: ''Messenger of Allah ﷺ accepted and attended invitations where bread made of barley, and stale fat a few days old was served (Without hesitation he accepted these invitations). Messenger of Allah ﷺ had pawned his armour to a Jew. Till the end of his life Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not possess a sufficient amount to release that armour'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَاصِلُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الأَعْلَى الْكُوفِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنِ الأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ، يُدْعَى إِلَى خُبْزِ الشَّعِيرِ، وَالإِهَالَةِ السَّنِخَةِ، فَيُجِيبُ وَلَقَدْ كَانَ لَهُ دِرْعٌ عِنْدَ يَهُودِيٍّ، فَمَا وَجَدَ مَا يَفُكُّهَا حَتَّى مَاتَ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 316

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 317
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports :
                      ''Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed haj on an old pack saddle. On it was a piece of cloth, the value of which was less than four Dirhams. (It may be possible that by 'on it' Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam himself is meant here. i.e. He covered himself with an ordinary sheet. The value of which did not reach four Dirhams. This explanation is most accepted by the respected teachers. The eleventh hadith in this chapter strengthens this). Messenger of Allah ﷺ was reciting this du'aa: 'Allah make this haj one that has no show or fame'".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports :
body: ''Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed haj on an old pack saddle. On it was a piece of cloth, the value of which was less than four Dirhams. (It may be possible that by 'on it' Messenger of Allah Sallallhu 'Alayhi Wasallam himself is meant here. i.e. He covered himself with an ordinary sheet. The value of which did not reach four Dirhams. This explanation is most accepted by the respected teachers. The eleventh hadith in this chapter strengthens this). Messenger of Allah ﷺ was reciting this du'aa: 'Allah make this haj one that has no show or fame'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الْحَفَرِيُّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ صَبِيحٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبَانَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: حَجَّ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، عَلَى رَحْلٍ رَثٍّ، وَعَلَيْهِ قَطِيفَةٌ، لا تُسَاوِي أَرْبَعَةَ دَرَاهِمَ، فَقَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْهُ حَجًّا، لا رِيَاءَ فِيهِ، وَلا سُمْعَةَ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 317

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 318
englishRaw:
Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "No one was more beloved to the Sahaabah than Messenger of Allah ﷺ. When they saw him they did not stand up, knowing that he did not approve of it".
narrator: Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "No one was more beloved to the Sahaabah than Messenger of Allah ﷺ. When they saw him they did not stand up, knowing that he did not approve of it".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَفَّانُ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: لَمْ يَكُنْ شَخْصٌ أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، قَالَ: وَكَانُوا إِذَا رَأَوْهُ لَمْ يَقُومُوا، لِمَا يَعْلَمُونَ مِنْ كَرَاهَتِهِ لِذَلِكَ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 318

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 319
englishRaw:
Imaam Hasan bin 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that I asked my (maternal) uncle Hind bin Abi Haalab, who usually described particulars and conditions of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. I was longing to hear something about it. On my asking, he described the mubaarak features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said:
                      'Messenger of Allah ﷺ had great qualities and attributes in him, others also held him in high esteem. His face glittered like the full moon'. He then described the complete features of Messenger of Allah ﷺ (as has been mentioned in the first chapter under hadith number seven). Imaam Hasan (may Allah be pleased with him) says. 'I did not mention this hadith (due to some reason) to Husayn ((may Allah be pleased with him)) for some time. Then I once narrated it to him whereupon I found that he had heard it before me. He had asked him (our uncle) already what I had asked. I also found that he had asked our father ('Ali (may Allah be pleased with him)) about Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Alayhi 'Wasallam entering and coming out of the house. He did not leave out anything about the ways and manners of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Imaam Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'I asked my father regarding the manner in which Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered the house?' He replied: 'When Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered the house he distributed his time into three portions; He spent a portion for Allah. (In devotion, performing salaah etc.); A portion towards his family (fulfilling their duties. i.e. laughing, speaking, enquiring about their welfare etc.); and a portion for himself (resting etc.). He distributed his personal portion in two, one for himself and one for the people, in such a manner that the near one's among the Sahaabah (may Allah be pleased with him)m came to visit him. Through these Sahaabah (may Allah be pleased with him)m he conveyed messages to the people. He did not conceal anything from them. From the portion of the unimah he adopted this method, that he gave preference to the Ahlul-Fadl (i.e. people of 'ilm and 'amal). He distributed this time according to their religious fadl. From among those who came, some had one requirement, some had two requirements, and some had many requirements. Messenger of Allah ﷺ fulfilled all their requirements. He busied them in things that benefited them and the entire ummah. When they questioned Messenger of Allah ﷺ on religious matters, he replied to them in a manner that benefited them. He used to say: 'Those that are present, should inform those that are not present regarding these beneficial and necessary matters'. He also used to say: 'Those people who for some reason (purdah, distance, shyness or awe) cannot put forward their requirements, you should inform me about their requirements, because, that person who informs a king of the need of another, who is unable to put forward that need, Allah Ta'aala will keep that person stead fast on the day of qiyaamah'. Only important and beneficial matters were discussed in his gathering. He happily listened to these matters from the Sahaabah (may Allah be pleased with him)m. Besides this there was no wasteful or non-beneficial talks in his assemblies. The Sahaabah (may Allah be pleased with him)m came to the assemblies of Messenger of Allah ﷺ for their religious needs, they did not depart before tasting something (by tasting, may be meant the acquiring of religious knowledge. It could mean 'Hissi' tasting too. Whatever Messenger of Allah ﷺ possessed, he offered it in humbleness. Whenever close friends gather, whatever is available is presented to them). The Sahaabah (may Allah be pleased with him)m returned from his assemblies as torch bearers of hidayah and goodness. (They spread. these teachings amongst others). Imaam Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) says: 'I asked (my father) regarding the coming out of Messenger of Allah ﷺ from the house'. He replied: 'Messenger of Allah ﷺ controlled his tongue and only spoke that which was necessary. He did not waste his time in useless conversations. He made those who came to visit him feel at home he did not make them feel scared or ill at ease. (i.e. When advising them, he did not scold them in a manner that they become scared, or tell them such things that would keep them away from the deen). He respected and honoured the respected ones of every nation. He also chose a leader for them. He warned the people of Allah's punishment (or he emphasised that the people be cautious). He also protected himself from troubling or harming people. Besides being cautious and commanding others to be cautious, he never lacked in courtesy towards others. He was concerned for the affairs of his friends, made himself aware about the relationships between them and rectified their faults. He praised good deeds and encouraged them. He explained the harmful effects of bad things and removed and stopped these. He followed the middle path in all matters. (He did not at times say this and at other times that). He did not neglect the guiding of people, it is possible that they became unmindful of their religious duties, or exceeded in a matter resulting in them becoming disheartened. For everything there was a special arrangement. He did not fall back in the truth, nor did he exceed the limits in this.Those who attended his gatherings were the best of people. The best person in the eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was the one who wished everybody well. The one with the highest status in the eyes of Messenger of Allah ﷺ was that person who considered, comforted and helped the creation the most'. Sayyidina Imaam Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) says: 'I then enquired from him regarding the assemblies of Messenger of Allah Sallallahu Wasallam'. He replied: 'He began and ended all his sitting with the dhikr of Allah. When he went to a place, he sat where he found a place, and also instructed the people to do so. They should not leap over peoples heads and go ahead. It is a different matter, that where Messenger of Allah ﷺ sat, that place became the focal point of the gathering. Messenger of Allah ﷺ fulfilled the rights of every person present. That means, whatever right was due in talking and showing happiness, was fulfilled by him, so much so, that every person present would think that Messenger of Allah ﷺ is honouring me the most. The person that came to sit by Messenger of Allah ﷺ or came to him for some purpose, Messenger of Allah ﷺ would remain seated till that person began to stand up. Whenever one asked him for something, he kindly fulfilled that request, and did not refuse it, (if he did not possess the thing) he would give a soft and humble answer. His cheerfulness and pleasant manner were for everybody. He was like a father to them. The whole creation was equal before him as far as rights were concerned. His gatherings were the gatherings of knowledge, modesty, patience and honesty. (i.e. These four things were attained there or are a description of his gatherings). Voices were not raised therein, nor was anyone degraded or disgraced. If anyone committed a fault, it was not made known publicly. All were regarded as equals amongst themselves. (A person was not regarded according to his lineage or genealogy). The virtues of one over the other was according to the taqwa (piety) possessed. The small ones were loved. The needy were given preference. Strangers and travellers were cared for"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جُمَيْعُ بْنُ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْعِجْلِيُّ، قَالَ: أَنْبَأَنَا رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي تَمِيمٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ أَبِي هَالَةَ زَوْجِ خَدِيجَةَ، يُكْنَى أَبَا عَبْدِ اللهِ، عَنِ ابْنٍ لأَبِي هَالَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ خَالِي هِنْدَ بْنَ أَبِي هَالَةَ، وَكَانَ وَصَّافًا عَنْ حِلْيَةِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، وَأَنَا أَشْتَهِي أَنْ يَصِفَ لِي مِنْهَا شَيْئًا، فَقَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ،: -. قَالَ: فَسَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ مَخْرَجِهِ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ فِيهِ؟ قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يَخْرِنُ لِسَانُهُ إِلا فِيمَا يَعْنِيهِ، وَيُؤَلِّفُهُمْ وَلا يُنَفِّرُهُمْ، وَيُكْرِمُ كَرَيمَ كُلِّ قَوْمٍ وَيُوَلِّيهِ عَلَيْهِمْ، وَيُحَذِّرُ النَّاسَ وَيَحْتَرِسُ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَطْوِيَ عَنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ بِشْرَهُ وَخُلُقَهُ، وَيَتَفَقَّدُ أَصْحَابَهُ، وَيَسْأَلُ النَّاسَ عَمَّا فِي النَّاسِ، وَيُحَسِّنُ الْحَسَنَ وَيُقَوِّيهِ، وَيُقَبِّحُ الْقَبِيحَ وَيُوَهِّيهِ، مُعْتَدِلُ الأَمْرِ غَيْرُ مُخْتَلِفٍ، لا يَغْفُلُ مَخَافَةَ أَنْ يَغْفُلُوا أَوْ يَمِيلُوا، لِكُلِّ حَالٍ عِنْدَهُ عَتَادٌ، لا يُقَصِّرُ عَنِ الْحَقِّ وَلا يُجَاوِزُهُ الَّذِينَ يَلُونَهُ مِنَ النَّاسِ خِيَارُهُمْ، أَفْضَلُهُمْ عِنْدَهُ أَعَمُّهُمْ نَصِيحَةً، وَأَعْظَمُهُمْ عِنْدَهُ مَنْزِلَةً أَحْسَنُهُمْ مُوَاسَاةً وَمُؤَازَرَةً قَالَ: فَسَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ مَجْلِسِهِ، فَقَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ لا يَقُومُ وَلا يَجَلِسُ، إِلا عَلَى ذِكْرٍ، وَإِذَا انْتَهَى إِلَى قَوْمٍ، جَلَسَ حَيْثُ يَنْتَهِي بِهِ الْمَجْلِسُ، وَيَأْمُرُ بِذَلِكَ، يُعْطِي كُلَّ جُلَسَائِهِ بِنَصِيبِهِ، لا يَحْسَبُ جَلِيسُهُ أَنَّ أَحَدًا أَكْرَمُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْهُ، مَنْ جَالَسَهُ أَوْ فَاوَضَهُ فِي حَاجَةٍ، صَابَرَهُ حَتَّى يَكُونَ هُوَ الْمُنْصَرِفُ عَنْهُ، وَمَنْ سَأَلَهُ حَاجَةً لَمْ يَرُدَّهُ إِلا بِهَا، أَوْ بِمَيْسُورٍ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ، قَدْ وَسِعَ النَّاسَ بَسْطُهُ وَخُلُقُهُ، فَصَارَ لَهُمْ أَبًا وَصَارُوا عِنْدَهُ فِي الْحَقِّ سَوَاءً، مَجْلِسُهُ مَجْلِسُ عِلْمٍ وَحِلْمٍ وَحَيَاءٍ وَأَمَانَةٍ وَصَبْرٍ، لا تُرْفَعُ فِيهِ الأَصْوَاتُ، وَلا تُؤْبَنُ فِيهِ الْحُرَمُ، وَلا تُثَنَّى فَلَتَاتُهُ، مُتَعَادِلِينَ، بَلْ كَانُوا يَتَفَاضَلُونَ فِيهِ بِالتَّقْوَى، مُتَوَاضِعِينَ يُوقِّرُونَ فِيهِ الْكَبِيرَ، وَيَرْحَمُونَ فِيهِ الصَّغِيرَ، وَيُؤْثِرُونَ ذَا الْحَاجَةِ، وَيَحْفَظُونَ الْغَرِيبَ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 319

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 320
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
                      "Even if I am given a foot of a goat as a present, I will accept it, and if I am invited to partake of it, I will surely accept that invitation".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
body: "Even if I am given a foot of a goat as a present, I will accept it, and if I am invited to partake of it, I will surely accept that invitation".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا بِشْرُ بْنُ الْمُفَضَّلِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدٌ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ: لوْ أُهْدِيَ إِلَيَّ كُرَاعٌ لَقَبِلتُ، وَلوْ دُعِيتُ عَلَيْهِ لأَجَبْتُ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 320

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 321
englishRaw:
Jaabir (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to visit me (while I was ill). He did not come on the back of a mule, or on a Turkish horse". (i.e. He did not come on defective or an expensive conveyance, but came on foot).
narrator: Jaabir (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to visit me (while I was ill). He did not come on the back of a mule, or on a Turkish horse". (i.e. He did not come on defective or an expensive conveyance, but came on foot).
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُنْكَدِرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: جَاءَنِي رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ لَيْسَ برَاكِبِ بَغْلٍ وَلا بِرْذَوْنٍ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 321

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 322
englishRaw:
Yusuf bin 'Abdullah bin Salaam (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah Sallallah 'Alayhi Wasallam chose for me the name Yusuf. He put me in his lap and passed his mibaarak hand on my head'".
narrator: Yusuf bin 'Abdullah bin Salaam (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah Sallallah 'Alayhi Wasallam chose for me the name Yusuf. He put me in his lap and passed his mibaarak hand on my head'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ، قَالَ: أَنْبَأَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي الْهَيْثَمِ الْعَطَّارُ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ يُوسُفَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ سَلامٍ، قَالَ: سَمَّانِي رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يُوسُفَ، وَأَقْعَدَنِي فِي حِجْرِهِ، وَمَسَحَ عَلَى رَأْسِي.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 322

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 323
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed haj on a saddled camel on which was a cloth, the value of which we think was four Dirhams. Messenger of Allah ﷺ was reciting this du'aa: 'O Allah, keep this haj free from show and fame'".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed haj on a saddled camel on which was a cloth, the value of which we think was four Dirhams. Messenger of Allah ﷺ was reciting this du'aa: 'O Allah, keep this haj free from show and fame'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الرَّبِيعُ وَهُوَ ابْنُ صَبِيحٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ الرَّقَاشِيُّ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، حَجَّ عَلَى رَحْلٍ رَثٍّ وَقَطِيفَةٍ، كُنَّا نَرَى ثَمَنَهَا أَرْبَعَةَ دَرَاهِمَ، فَلَمَّا اسْتَوَتْ بِهِ رَاحِلَتُهُ، قَالَ: لَبَّيْكَ بِحَجَّةٍ لا سُمْعَةَ فِيهَا وَلا رِيَاءَ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 323

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 324
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that a tailor invited Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Thareed was served, in which dubbaa (dodhi -gourd) was added. As Messenger of Allah ﷺ loved dubbaa (dodhi-gourd) he began eating it. Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "After that no food was prepared for me, wherein if gourd could be added, it was added"
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that a tailor invited Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Thareed was served, in which dubbaa (dodhi -gourd) was added. As Messenger of Allah ﷺ loved dubbaa (dodhi-gourd) he began eating it. Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "After that no food was prepared for me, wherein if gourd could be added, it was added"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْمَرٌ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ الْبُنَانِيِّ، وَعَاصِمٍ الأَحْوَلِ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، أَنَّ رَجُلا خَيَّاطًا دَعَا رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، فَقَرَّبَ مِنْهُ ثَرِيدًا عَلَيْهِ دُبَّاءُ، قَالَ: فَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يَأْخُذُ الدُّبَّاءَ، وَكَانَ يُحِبُّ الدُّبَّاءَ، قَالَ ثَابِتٌ: فَسَمِعْتُ أَنَسًا، يَقُولُ: فَمَا صُنِعَ لِي طَعَامٌ، أَقْدَرُ عَلَى أَنْ يُصْنَعَ فِيهِ دُبَّاءُ، إِلا صُنِعَ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 324

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 325
englishRaw:
'Amrah (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that someone asked 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). "What was the usual practice of Messenger of Allah ﷺ at home?" She replied:
                      "He was a human from among other humans. He himself removed the lice from his clothing, milked his goats, and did all his work himself".
narrator: 'Amrah (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that someone asked 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her). "What was the usual practice of Messenger of Allah ﷺ at home?" She replied:
body: "He was a human from among other humans. He himself removed the lice from his clothing, milked his goats, and did all his work himself".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَمْرَةَ، قَالَتْ: قِيلَ لِعَائِشَةَ: مَاذَا كَانَ يَعْمَلُ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ فِي بَيْتِهِ؟ قَالَتْ: كَانَ بَشَرًا مِنَ الْبَشَرِ، يَفْلِي ثَوْبَهُ، وَيَحْلُبُ شَاتَهُ، وَيَخْدُمُ نَفْسَهُ.
references: Book of The Humbleness Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 325

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 326
englishRaw: Khaarijah bin Zayd bin Thaabit (may Allah be pleased with him) says that a group came to Zayd bin Thaabit (his father) and requested him to describe to them some facts about Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He replied. "What can I describe to you of Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Alayhi 'Wasallam. (It is beyond my means to describe them). I was the neighbour of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. (Therefore he was mostly present and knew many facts. He was also a writer of the wahi-revelation). When wahi was revealed to Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he sent for me, I came and wrote it. (Messenger of Allah ﷺ always showed kindness, and made us feel at ease). Whatever we discussed, he discussed the same. If we discussed some worldly affairs, he also spoke of it. (It was not that he only spoke about the hereafter to us, and despised the talking of worldly affairs). When we spoke of the hereafter, he too spoke of the hereafter. When we began speaking of the hereafter, he described its events etc in detail). 'When we spoke of food, Messenger of Allah ﷺ also spoke of it. (Its etiquette, benefits, tasty foods, foods that are harmful, etc. In the previous chapters many such commands of Messenger of Allah ﷺ are mentioned. i.e. 'What a wonderful curry vinegar is'. 'Use olive oil, it is from a mubaarak tree'. etc.). All this I am saying are facts on Messenger of Allah ﷺ'".
body: Khaarijah bin Zayd bin Thaabit (may Allah be pleased with him) says that a group came to Zayd bin Thaabit (his father) and requested him to describe to them some facts about Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He replied. "What can I describe to you of Messenger of Allah Sallallahu 'Alayhi 'Wasallam. (It is beyond my means to describe them). I was the neighbour of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. (Therefore he was mostly present and knew many facts. He was also a writer of the wahi-revelation). When wahi was revealed to Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he sent for me, I came and wrote it. (Messenger of Allah ﷺ always showed kindness, and made us feel at ease). Whatever we discussed, he discussed the same. If we discussed some worldly affairs, he also spoke of it. (It was not that he only spoke about the hereafter to us, and despised the talking of worldly affairs). When we spoke of the hereafter, he too spoke of the hereafter. When we began speaking of the hereafter, he described its events etc in detail). 'When we spoke of food, Messenger of Allah ﷺ also spoke of it. (Its etiquette, benefits, tasty foods, foods that are harmful, etc. In the previous chapters many such commands of Messenger of Allah ﷺ are mentioned. i.e. 'What a wonderful curry vinegar is'. 'Use olive oil, it is from a mubaarak tree'. etc.). All this I am saying are facts on Messenger of Allah ﷺ'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّاسُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الدُّورِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ الْمُقْرِئِ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا لَيْثُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبُو عُثْمَانَ الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي الْوَلِيدِ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَارِجَةَ، عَنْ خَارِجَةَ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، قَالَ: دَخَلَ نَفَرٌ عَلَى زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، فَقَالُوا لَهُ: حَدِّثْنَا أَحَادِيثَ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، قَالَ: مَاذَا أُحَدِّثُكُمْ؟ كُنْتُ جَارَهُ فَكَانَ إِذَا نَزَلَ عَلَيْهِ الْوَحْيُ بَعَثَ إِلَيَّ فَكَتَبْتُهُ لَهُ، فَكُنَّا إِذَا ذَكَرْنَا الدُّنْيَا ذَكَرَهَا مَعَنَا، وَإِذَا ذَكَرْنَا الآخِرَةَ ذَكَرَهَا مَعَنَا، وَإِذَا ذَكَرْنَا الطَّعَامَ ذَكَرَهُ مَعَنَا، فَكُلُّ هَذَا أُحَدِّثُكُمْ عَنِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 326

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 327
englishRaw:
'Amr ibnul 'Aas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "(Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave attention, spoke and showed love to the worst person of a nation. So that, the person may feel he is being given special attention). He used to give attention, and spoke to me also in a manner, that I began to feel that I was the best among the community. (Therefore one day) I asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, am I better or is Abubakr better?' He replied: 'Abubakr'. I then asked: 'Am I better, or 'Umar?' He replied. "Umar'. I asked: 'Am I better or 'Uthmaan?' He replied: 'Uthmaan'. When I asked him these questions, Messenger of Allah ﷺ told me the truth. (He did not tell me I was better to keep me happy. Afterwards I felt ashamed of myself on this deed). I felt I should not have asked such a question".
narrator: 'Amr ibnul 'Aas (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "(Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave attention, spoke and showed love to the worst person of a nation. So that, the person may feel he is being given special attention). He used to give attention, and spoke to me also in a manner, that I began to feel that I was the best among the community. (Therefore one day) I asked: 'O Messenger of Allah, am I better or is Abubakr better?' He replied: 'Abubakr'. I then asked: 'Am I better, or 'Umar?' He replied. "Umar'. I asked: 'Am I better or 'Uthmaan?' He replied: 'Uthmaan'. When I asked him these questions, Messenger of Allah ﷺ told me the truth. (He did not tell me I was better to keep me happy. Afterwards I felt ashamed of myself on this deed). I felt I should not have asked such a question".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مُوسَى، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ بُكَيْرٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ زِيَادِ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ كَعْبٍ الْقُرَظِيِّ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ الْعَاصِ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، يُقْبِلُ بِوَجْهِهِ وَحَدِيثِهِ عَلَى أَشَرِّ الْقَوْمِ، يَتَأَلَّفُهُمْ بِذَلِكَ فَكَانَ يُقْبِلُ بِوَجْهِهِ وَحَدِيثِهِ عَلَيَّ، حَتَّى ظَنَنْتُ أَنِّي خَيْرُ الْقَوْمِ، فَقُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، أَنَا خَيْرٌ أَوْ أَبُو بَكْرٍ؟ فَقَالَ: أَبُو بَكْرٍ، فَقُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، أَنَا خَيْرٌ أَوْ عُمَرُ؟ فَقَالَ: عُمَرُ، فَقُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، أَنَا خَيْرٌ أَوْ عُثْمَانُ؟ فَقَالَ: عُثْمَانُ، فَلَمَّا سَأَلْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ، فَصَدَقَنِي فَلَوَدِدْتُ أَنِّي لَمْ أَكُنْ سَأَلْتُهُ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 327

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 328
englishRaw:
Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "I remained in the service (of Messenger of Allah ﷺ for ten years. He never once told me 'Oof'. When I did something, he never asked me, why did you do so? When I did not do a certain task, he never asked me why I did not do it. Messenger of Allah ﷺ had the best character among all people. (and also possessed the most excellent features, so much so,) that I never felt a silk cloth, nor pure silk, nor any other thing softer than the palm of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Nor did I smell any musk or any other fragrance, more sweet smelling than the sweat of Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
narrator: Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "I remained in the service (of Messenger of Allah ﷺ for ten years. He never once told me 'Oof'. When I did something, he never asked me, why did you do so? When I did not do a certain task, he never asked me why I did not do it. Messenger of Allah ﷺ had the best character among all people. (and also possessed the most excellent features, so much so,) that I never felt a silk cloth, nor pure silk, nor any other thing softer than the palm of Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Nor did I smell any musk or any other fragrance, more sweet smelling than the sweat of Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ الضُّبَعِيُّ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: خَدَمْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ، فَمَا قَالَ لِي أُفٍّ قَطُّ، وَمَا قَالَ لِشَيْءٍ صَنَعْتُهُ، لِمَ صَنَعْتَهُ، وَلا لِشَيْءٍ تَرَكْتُهُ، لِمَ تَرَكْتَهُ؟ وَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، مِنْ أَحْسَنِ النَّاسِ خُلُقًا، وَلا مَسَسْتُ خَزًّا وَلا حَرِيرًا، وَلا شَيْئًا كَانَ أَلْيَنَ مِنْ كَفِّ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، وَلا شَمَمْتُ مِسْكًا قَطُّ، وَلا عِطْرًا كَانَ أَطْيَبَ مِنْ عَرَقِ رسول الله ﷺ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 328

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 329
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "A person who was wearing yellow coloured clothing was sitting by Messenger of Allah ﷺ. It was the habit of Messenger of Allah ﷺ not to bluntly express things he did not approve of. (Therefore he kept silent). When that person left, Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to those present 'It would have been better if you had told him not to wear yellow clothing'".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "A person who was wearing yellow coloured clothing was sitting by Messenger of Allah ﷺ. It was the habit of Messenger of Allah ﷺ not to bluntly express things he did not approve of. (Therefore he kept silent). When that person left, Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to those present 'It would have been better if you had told him not to wear yellow clothing'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، وَأَحَمْدُ بْنُ عَبْدَةَ هُوَ الضَّبِّيُّ، وَالْمَعْنَى وَاحِدٌ، قَالا: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ سَلْمٍ الْعَلَوِيِّ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ عِنْدَهُ رَجُلٌ بِهِ أَثَرُ صُفْرَةٍ، قَالَ: وَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، لا يكَادُ يُواجِهُ أَحَدًا بِشَيْءٍ يَكْرَهُهُ، فَلَمَّا قَامَ، قَالَ لِلْقَوْمِ: لَوْ قُلْتُمْ لَهُ يَدَعُ هَذِهِ الصُّفْرَةَ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 329

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 330
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports, that:
                      "It was not the nature of Messenger of Allah ﷺ to talk indecently, nor did he engage himself in the use of obscene language. Nor did he shout and talk in the bazaars (which is against dignity). He did not avenge a bad deed with a bad one, but forgave it, and thereafter did not even mention it".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports, that:
body: "It was not the nature of Messenger of Allah ﷺ to talk indecently, nor did he engage himself in the use of obscene language. Nor did he shout and talk in the bazaars (which is against dignity). He did not avenge a bad deed with a bad one, but forgave it, and thereafter did not even mention it".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللهِ الْجَدَلِيِّ وَاسْمُهُ عَبْدُ بْنُ عَبْدٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، أَنَّهَا قَالَتْ: لَمْ يَكُنْ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، فَاحِشًا، وَلا مُتَفَحِّشًا وَلا صَخَّابًا فِي الأَسْوَاقِ، وَلا يَجْزِئُ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ السَّيِّئَةَ، وَلَكِنْ يَعْفُو وَيَصْفَحُ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 330

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 331
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not hit anything with his mubaarak hands, besides the time when he made jihaad in the Path of Allah. He did not hit a servant nor a women (wife, slave girl etc.)".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not hit anything with his mubaarak hands, besides the time when he made jihaad in the Path of Allah. He did not hit a servant nor a women (wife, slave girl etc.)".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ الْهَمْدَانِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: مَا ضَرَبَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، بِيَدِهِ شَيْئًا قَطُّ، إِلا أَنْ يُجَاهِدَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ، وَلا ضَرَبَ خَادِمًا َوِلا امْرَأَةً.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 331

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 332
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
                      "I have never seen Messenger of Allah ﷺ avenge himself for a personal affliction, but if one transgressed a prohibited thing from those prohibited by Allah, (To commit a haraam act. The commentators on hadith say the rights of man are also included) then there was no one more angry than Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Whenever Messenger of Allah ﷺ was given a choice between two things, he always chose the one that was simple, if it did not lead to any type of sin".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
body: "I have never seen Messenger of Allah ﷺ avenge himself for a personal affliction, but if one transgressed a prohibited thing from those prohibited by Allah, (To commit a haraam act. The commentators on hadith say the rights of man are also included) then there was no one more angry than Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Whenever Messenger of Allah ﷺ was given a choice between two things, he always chose the one that was simple, if it did not lead to any type of sin".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدَةَ الضَّبِّيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا فُضَيْلُ بْنُ عِيَاضٍ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتْ: مَا رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ مُنْتَصِرًا مِنْ مَظْلَمَةٍ ظُلِمَهَا قَطُّ، مَا لَمْ يُنْتَهَكْ مِنْ مَحَارِمِ اللهِ تَعَالَى شَيْءٌ، فَإِذَا انْتُهِكَ مِنْ مَحَارِمِ اللهِ شَيْءٌ كَانَ مِنْ أَشَدِّهِمْ فِي ذَلِكَ غَضَبًا، وَمَا خُيِّرَ بَيْنَ أَمْرَيْنِ، إِلا اخْتَارَ أَيْسَرَهُمَا، مَا لَمْ يَكُنْ مَأْثَمًا.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 332

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 333
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports. "A person asked permission to present himself before Messenger of Allah ﷺ while I was with him. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
                      'What a bad person is he among his community'. After saying this, he gave him permission to enter. After the person entered, he spoke very softly to him. When the person left I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, you said what you said before he entered, then you spoke so softly to him,. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Aisha, the worst person is that who stops speaking to one because of his indecency'".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports. "A person asked permission to present himself before Messenger of Allah ﷺ while I was with him. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:
body: 'What a bad person is he among his community'. After saying this, he gave him permission to enter. After the person entered, he spoke very softly to him. When the person left I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, you said what you said before he entered, then you spoke so softly to him,. Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Aisha, the worst person is that who stops speaking to one because of his indecency'".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عُمَرَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُنْكَدِرِ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، قَالَتِ: اسْتَأْذَنَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ، وَأَنَا عِنْدَهُ، فَقَالَ: بِئْسَ ابْنُ الْعَشِيرَةِ أَوْ أَخُو الْعَشِيرَةِ، ثُمَّ أَذِنَ لَهُ، فَأَلانَ لَهُ الْقَوْلَ، فَلَمَّا خَرَجَ، قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، قُلْتَ مَا قُلْتَ ثُمَّ أَلَنْتَ لَهُ الْقَوْلَ؟ فَقَالَ: يَا عَائِشَةُ، إِنَّ مِنْ شَرِّ النَّاسِ مَنْ تَرَكَهُ النَّاسُ أَوْ وَدَعَهُ النَّاسُ اتِّقَاءَ فُحْشِهِ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 333

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 334
englishRaw:
Imaam Hasan (may Allah be pleased with him) says, (my younger brother) Husayn said:
                      "I asked my father (Sayyidina 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him)) about the conduct of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in his assemblies' He replied.. 'Messenger of Allah ﷺ was always happy and easy mannered. There was always a smile and a sign of happiness on his blessed face. He was soft-natured and when the people needed his approval, he easily gave consent. He did not speak in a harsh tone nor was he stone-hearted. He did not scream while speaking, nor was he rude or spoke indecently. He did not seek other's faults. He never over-praised anything nor exceeded in joking, nor was he a miser. He kept away from undesirable language and did not make as if he did not hear anything. If he did not agree with the next person's wish he did not make that person feel disheartened, nor did he promise anything to that person. He completely kept himself away from three things: from arguments, pride and senseless utterances. He prohibited people from three things. He did not disgrace or insult anyone, nor look for the faults of others, he only spoke that from which thawaab and reward was attained. When he spoke, those present bowed their heads in such a manner, as if birds were sitting on their heads. (They did not shift about, as birds will fly away on the slightest move). When he completed his talks, the others would begin speaking. (No one would speak while Messenger of Allah ﷺ spoke. Whatever one wanted to say, it would be said after he had completed speaking). They did not argue before him regarding anything. Whenever one spoke to him the other would keep quiet and listen till he would finish. The speech of every person was as if the first person was speaking. (They gave attention to what every person said. It was not as is generally found that in the beginning people pay full attention, and if the talk is lengthened they became bored, and begin to pay less attention). When all laughed for something, he would laugh too. The things that surprised the people, he would also show his surprise regarding that. (He would not sit quietly and keep himself aloof from everyone, but made himself part of the gathering). He exercised patience at the harshness and indecent questions of a traveller. (Villagers usually ask irrelevant questions. They do not show courtesy and ask all types of questions. Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not reprimand them but exercised patience). The Sahaabah would bring travellers to his assemblies (so that they themselves could benefit from the various types of questions asked by these people, and also hear some questions regarding which they themselves, due to etiquette, would not ask). Messenger of Allah ﷺ' would say: 'When you see a person in need, then always 'help that person'. (If someone praised him, he would detest it). If someone, by way of thanks praised him, he would remain silent, (because it is necessary that one 'thank a person for a good favour or good deed. It is like one fulfilling one's duty. Some of the 'ulama have translated this as: 'If one did not exceed in praising him, he would keep silent'. That means if he exceeded he would prohibit him). He did not interrupt someone talking and did not begin speaking when someone else was busy speaking. If one exceeded the limits he would stop him or would get up and leave (so that that person would stop)"
narrator: Imaam Hasan (may Allah be pleased with him) says, (my younger brother) Husayn said:
body: "I asked my father (Sayyidina 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him)) about the conduct of Messenger of Allah ﷺ in his assemblies' He replied.. 'Messenger of Allah ﷺ was always happy and easy mannered. There was always a smile and a sign of happiness on his blessed face. He was soft-natured and when the people needed his approval, he easily gave consent. He did not speak in a harsh tone nor was he stone-hearted. He did not scream while speaking, nor was he rude or spoke indecently. He did not seek other's faults. He never over-praised anything nor exceeded in joking, nor was he a miser. They did not argue before him regarding anything. Whenever one spoke to him the other would keep quiet and listen till he would finish. When all laughed for something, he would laugh too. The things that surprised the people, he would also show his surprise regarding that. (He would not sit quietly and keep himself aloof from everyone, but made himself part of the gathering). He exercised patience at the harshness and indecent questions of a traveller. (Villagers usually ask irrelevant questions. They do not show courtesy and ask all types of questions. Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not reprimand them but exercised patience). Some of the 'ulama have translated this as: 'If one did not exceed in praising him, he would keep silent'. That means if he exceeded he would prohibit him). He did not interrupt someone talking and did not begin speaking when someone else was busy speaking. If one exceeded the limits he would stop him or would get up and leave (so that that person would stop)"
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ وَكِيعٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جُمَيْعُ بْنُ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْعِجْلِيُّ، قَالَ: أَنْبَأَنَا رَجُلٌ مِنْ بَنِي تَمِيمٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ أَبِي هَالَةَ زَوْجِ خَدِيجَةَ، وَيُكْنَى أَبَا عَبْدِ اللهِ، عَنِ ابْنٍ لأَبِي هَالَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: قَالَ الْحُسَيْنُ: سَأَلْتُ أَبي عَنْ سِيرَةِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فِي جُلَسَائِهِ، فَقَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، دَائِمَ الْبِشْرِ، سَهْلَ الْخُلُقِ، لَيِّنَ الْجَانِبِ، لَيْسَ بِفَظٍّ وَلا غَلِيظٍ، وَلا صَخَّابٍ وَلا فَحَّاشٍ، وَلا عَيَّابٍ وَلا مُشَاحٍ، يَتَغَافَلُ عَمَّا لا يَشْتَهِي، وَلا يُؤْيِسُ مِنْهُ رَاجِيهِ وَلا يُخَيَّبُ فِيهِ، قَدْ تَرَكَ نَفْسَهُ مِنْ ثَلاثٍ: الْمِرَاءِ، وَالإِكْثَارِ، وَمَا لا يَعْنِيهِ، وَتَرَكَ النَّاسَ مِنْ ثَلاثٍ: كَانَ لا يَذُمُّ أَحَدًا، وَلا يَعِيبُهُ، وَلا يَطْلُبُ عَوْرتَهُ، وَلا يَتَكَلَّمُ إِلا فِيمَا رَجَا ثَوَابَهُ، وَإِذَا تَكَلَّمَ أَطْرَقَ جُلَسَاؤُهُ، كَأَنَّمَا عَلَى رُؤُوسِهِمُ الطَّيْرُ، فَإِذَا سَكَتَ تَكَلَّمُوا لا يَتَنَازَعُونَ عِنْدَهُ الْحَدِيثَ، وَمَنْ تَكَلَّمَ عِنْدَهُ أَنْصَتُوا لَهُ حَتَّى يَفْرُغَ، حَدِيثُهُمْ عِنْدَهُ حَدِيثُ أَوَّلِهِمْ، يَضْحَكُ مِمَّا يَضْحَكُونَ مِنْهُ، وَيَتَعَجَّبُ مِمَّا يَتَعَجَّبُونَ مِنْهُ، وَيَصْبِرُ لِلْغَرِيبِ عَلَى الْجَفْوَةِ فِي مَنْطِقِهِ وَمَسْأَلَتِهِ، حَتَّى إِنْ كَانَ أَصْحَابُهُ، وَيَقُولُ: إِذَا رَأَيْتُمْ طَالِبَ حَاجَةٍ يِطْلُبُهَا فَأَرْفِدُوهُ، وَلا يَقْبَلُ الثَّنَاءَ إِلا مِنْ مُكَافِئٍ وَلا يَقْطَعُ عَلَى أَحَدٍ حَدِيثَهُ حَتَّى يَجُوزَ فَيَقْطَعُهُ بِنَهْيٍ أَوْ قِيَامٍ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 334

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 335
englishRaw: Jaabir (may Allah be pleased with him) says. "Messenger of Allah ﷺ never said 'No' to a request of a person".
body: Jaabir (may Allah be pleased with him) says. "Messenger of Allah ﷺ never said 'No' to a request of a person".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَشَّارٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُنْكَدِرِ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ جَابِرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللهِ، يَقُولُ: مَا سُئِلَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، شَيْئًا قَطُّ فَقَالَ: لا.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 335

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 336
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was the most generous among people in performing good deeds (No one could compare with him in generosity. He himself led a simple life, but in giving he would put a king to shame. At a time of great need a woman presented him a sheet, and he wore it as he was in need of it. A person came to him, and asked him for it, he presented the sheet to that person. Taking of loans and fulfilling the needs of others, when the creditors came, and if something had come from somewhere, he would pay the debts, and did not go home till everything was given to the needy. There exists many incidents of this nature, so much so that it is not possible to enumerate them). Particularly in the month of Ramadaan, he would be more generous till the month ended. (His generosity in this month exceeded all the other months). In this month when Jibra-eel 'Alayhis Salaam came and recited the Qur-aan to Messenger of Allah ﷺ, at that time his generosity exceeded the wind that brings forth heavy rains".
narrator: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ was the most generous among people in performing good deeds (No one could compare with him in generosity. He himself led a simple life, but in giving he would put a king to shame. At a time of great need a woman presented him a sheet, and he wore it as he was in need of it. A person came to him, and asked him for it, he presented the sheet to that person. Taking of loans and fulfilling the needs of others, when the creditors came, and if something had come from somewhere, he would pay the debts, and did not go home till everything was given to the needy. There exists many incidents of this nature, so much so that it is not possible to enumerate them). Particularly in the month of Ramadaan, he would be more generous till the month ended. (His generosity in this month exceeded all the other months). In this month when Jibra-eel 'Alayhis Salaam came and recited the Qur-aan to Messenger of Allah ﷺ, at that time his generosity exceeded the wind that brings forth heavy rains".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللهِ بْنُ عِمْرَانَ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ الْقُرَشِيُّ الْمَكِّيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، أَجْوَدَ النَّاسِ بِالْخَيْرِ، وَكَانَ أَجْوَدَ مَا يَكُونُ فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ، حَتَّى يَنْسَلِخَ، فَيَأْتِيهِ جِبْرِيلُ، فَيَعْرِضُ عَلَيْهِ الْقُرْآنَ، فَإِذَا لَقِيَهُ جِبْرِيلُ كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، أَجْوَدَ بِالْخَيْرِ مِنَ الرِّيحِ الْمُرْسَلَةِ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 336

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 337
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
                      "Messenger of Allah. ﷺ did not store anything for the next day".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) says:
body: "Messenger of Allah. ﷺ did not store anything for the next day".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا قُتَيْبَةُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ، لا يَدَّخِرُ شَيْئًا لِغَدٍ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 337

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 338
englishRaw:
'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that once a needy person came to ask Messenger of Allah ﷺ for his need. Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied:
                      "I do not have anything at present. Go and purchase something on my behalf. When something arrives I will pay for it". 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said: "O Messenger of Allah, whatever you possessed you have already given away. Allah Ta'aala did not make you responsible for that which is not in your means". Messenger of Allah ﷺ felt annoyed at this saying of 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him). Thereupon a person from among, the Ansaar said: "O Rasul, of Allah, spend whatever you wish, and do not fear any lessening from the Lord of the 'Arsh (Throne)". (That Great Deity that is the Lord of the 'Arsh, nothing will decrease in His Bounties by giving you). Messenger of Allah ﷺ smiled and the happiness could be seen on his mubaarak face due to the saying of the Ansaari. Messenger of Allah ﷺ than said: "Allah Ta'aala has commanded me to do this".
narrator: 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports that once a needy person came to ask Messenger of Allah ﷺ for his need. Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied:
body: "I do not have anything at present. Go and purchase something on my behalf. When something arrives I will pay for it". 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said: "O Messenger of Allah, whatever you possessed you have already given away. Allah Ta'aala did not make you responsible for that which is not in your means". Messenger of Allah ﷺ felt annoyed at this saying of 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him). Thereupon a person from among, the Ansaar said: "O Rasul, of Allah, spend whatever you wish, and do not fear any lessening from the Lord of the 'Arsh (Throne)". (That Great Deity that is the Lord of the 'Arsh, nothing will decrease in His Bounties by giving you). Messenger of Allah ﷺ smiled and the happiness could be seen on his mubaarak face due to the saying of the Ansaari. Messenger of Allah ﷺ than said: "Allah Ta'aala has commanded me to do this".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ مُوسَى بْنِ أَبِي عَلْقَمَةَ الْمَدِينِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، أَنَّ رَجُلا جَاءَ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَسَأَلَهُ أَنْ يُعْطِيَهُ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: مَا عِنْدِي شَيْءٌ، وَلَكِنِ ابْتَعْ عَلَيَّ، فَإِذَا جَاءَنِي شَيْءٌ قَضَيْتُهُ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، قَدْ أَعْطَيْتُهُ فَمَّا كَلَّفَكَ اللَّهُ مَا لا تَقْدِرُ عَلَيْهِ، فَكَرِهَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ قَوْلَ عُمَرَ، فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الأَنْصَارِ: يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ، أَنْفِقْ وَلا تَخَفْ مِنْ ذِي الْعَرْشِ إِقْلالا، فَتَبَسَّمَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ وَعُرِفَ فِي وَجْهِهِ الْبِشْرَ لِقَوْلِ الأَنْصَارِيِّ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: بِهَذَا أُمِرْتُ.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 338

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 339
englishRaw:
Rubayyi' bint Mu'awwidh bin 'Af-raa (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
                      "I brought to Messenger of Allah ﷺ a tray full of dates, and some small cucumbers. Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave me a handful of jewellery".
narrator: Rubayyi' bint Mu'awwidh bin 'Af-raa (may Allah be pleased with her) says:
body: "I brought to Messenger of Allah ﷺ a tray full of dates, and some small cucumbers. Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave me a handful of jewellery".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَقِيلٍ، عَنِ الرُّبَيِّعِ بِنْتِ مُعَوِّذِ بْنِ عَفْرَاءَ، قَالَتْ: أَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، بِقِنَاعٍ مِنْ رُطَبٍ وَأَجْرٍ زُغْبٍ، فَأَعْطَانِي مِلْءَ كَفِّهِ حُلِيًّا وَذَهَبًا.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 339

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 340
englishRaw: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that Messenger of Allah ﷺ accepted gifts, and also gave presents in return.
body: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports that Messenger of Allah ﷺ accepted gifts, and also gave presents in return.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ خَشْرَمٍ، وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، قَالُوا: حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، كَانَ يَقْبَلُ الْهَدِيَّةَ، وَيُثِيبُ عَلَيْهَا.
references: Book of Noble Character And Habits Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 340

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 341
englishRaw: Abu Sa'eed Khudari (may Allah be pleased with him) says that Messenger of Allah ﷺ was more bashful than a virgin girl in her veil (purdah). When Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not like something, it could be seen on his face. (Because of his excessive Modesty he did not mention it).
body: Abu Sa'eed Khudari (may Allah be pleased with him) says that Messenger of Allah ﷺ was more bashful than a virgin girl in her veil (purdah). When Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not like something, it could be seen on his face. (Because of his excessive Modesty he did not mention it).
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَبْدَ اللهِ بْنَ أَبِي عُتْبَةَ، يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ، قَالَ: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ أَشدَّ حَيَاءً مِنَ الْعَذرَاءِ فِي خِدْرِهَا، وَكَانَ إِذَا كَرِهَ شَيْئًا عَرَفْنَاهُ فِي وَجْهِهِ.
references: Book of Modesty Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 341

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 342
englishRaw:
'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
                      (because of the excessive modesty of Messenger of Allah ﷺ) "I did not have the courage to see the private parts of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, or that I never saw the private parts of Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
narrator: 'Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) reports:
body: (because of the excessive modesty of Messenger of Allah ﷺ) "I did not have the courage to see the private parts of Messenger of Allah ﷺ, or that I never saw the private parts of Messenger of Allah ﷺ".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلانَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ الْخَطْمِيِّ، عَنْ مَوْلًى لِعَائِشَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ: مَا نَظَرْتُ إِلَى فَرْجِ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ أَوْ قَالَتْ: مَا رَأَيْتُ فَرْجَ رَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ قَطُّ.
references: Book of Modesty Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 342

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 343
englishRaw:
Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) was asked regarding the payment to a hajjaam (cupper). (Is it permissible or not?) Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) replied:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ took the treatment of cupping which was administered by Abu Taybah (may Allah be pleased with him), he was given two saa' food (in a narration it is mentioned that dates were given), and Messenger of Allah ﷺ interceded on his behalf to his master that the stipulated amount he was responsible for be made less. He also said this, that cupping is the best of medicine".
narrator: Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) was asked regarding the payment to a hajjaam (cupper). (Is it permissible or not?) Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) replied:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ took the treatment of cupping which was administered by Abu Taybah (may Allah be pleased with him), he was given two saa' food (in a narration it is mentioned that dates were given), and Messenger of Allah ﷺ interceded on his behalf to his master that the stipulated amount he was responsible for be made less. He also said this, that cupping is the best of medicine".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حُجْرٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ أَنَسُ بْنُ مَالِكٍ عَنْ كَسْبِ الْحَجَّامِ، فَقَالَ: احْتَجَمَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ، حَجَمَهُ أَبُو طَيْبَةَ، فَأَمَرَ لَهُ بِصَاعَيْنِ مِنْ طَعَامٍ، وَكَلَّمَ أَهْلَهُ فَوَضَعُوا عَنْهُ مِنْ خَرَاجِهِ، وَقَالَ: إِنَّ أَفْضَلَ مَا تَدَاوَيْتَمْ بِهِ الْحِجَامَةُ، أَوْ إِنَّ مِنْ أَمْثَلِ دَوَائِكُمُ الْحِجَامَةَ.
references: Book of Hajaamah (Cupping-Cautering) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 343

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 344
englishRaw:
'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once took the treatment of cupping and asked me to pay its fees. I paid the hajjaam (cupper) his fees".
narrator: 'Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ once took the treatment of cupping and asked me to pay its fees. I paid the hajjaam (cupper) his fees".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو دَاوُدَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَرْقَاءُ بْنُ عُمَرَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الأَعْلَى، عَنْ أَبِي جَمِيلَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ: أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، احْتَجَمَ وَأَمَرَنِي فَأَعْطَيْتُ الْحَجَّامَ أَجْرَهُ.
references: Book of Hajaamah (Cupping-Cautering) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 344

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 345
englishRaw: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) said that Messenger of Allah ﷺ took the treatment of cupping on both sides of his neck and between his shoulders, and paid the cupper his fees. If it had been haraam, he would not have paid it.
body: Ibn 'Abbaas (may Allah be pleased with him) said that Messenger of Allah ﷺ took the treatment of cupping on both sides of his neck and between his shoulders, and paid the cupper his fees. If it had been haraam, he would not have paid it.
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ الْهَمْدَانِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: إِنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ احْتَجَمَ فِي الأَخْدَعَيْنِ، وَبَيْنَ الْكَتِفَيْنِ، وَأَعْطَى الْحَجَّامَ أَجْرَهُ، وَلَوْ كَانَ حَرَامًا لَمْ يُعْطِهِ.
references: Book of Hajaamah (Cupping-Cautering) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 345

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 346
englishRaw:
Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ called a hajjaam, who treated Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ inquired from him what tax or duty did he have to pay daily? He replied, three saa'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ had it reduced to two saa', and gave him his remuneration".
narrator: Ibn 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ called a hajjaam, who treated Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Messenger of Allah ﷺ inquired from him what tax or duty did he have to pay daily? He replied, three saa'. Messenger of Allah ﷺ had it reduced to two saa', and gave him his remuneration".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هَارُونُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، دَعَا حَجَّامًا فَحَجَمَهُ وَسَأَلَهُ: كَمْ خَرَاجُكَ؟ فَقَالَ: ثَلاثَةُ آصُعٍ، فَوَضَعَ عَنْهُ صَاعًا وَأَعْطَاهُ أَجْرَهُ.
references: Book of Hajaamah (Cupping-Cautering) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 346

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 347
englishRaw:
Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) said:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ used the treatment of cupping on both sides of his mubaarak neck and between both shoulders, and generally took this treatment on the seventeenth, nineteenth or the twenty first of the (lunar) month".
narrator: Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) said:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ used the treatment of cupping on both sides of his mubaarak neck and between both shoulders, and generally took this treatment on the seventeenth, nineteenth or the twenty first of the (lunar) month".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْقُدُّوسِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْعَطَّارُ الْبَصْرِيُّ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ عَاصِمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هَمَّامٌ، وَجَرِيرُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا قَتَادَةُ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ ﷺ يَحْتَجِمُ فِي الأَخْدَعَيْنِ وَالْكَاهِلِ، وَكَانَ يَحْتَجِمُ لِسَبْعَ عَشْرَةَ، وَتِسْعَ عَشْرَةَ، وَإِحْدَى وَعِشْرِينَ.
references: Book of Hajaamah (Cupping-Cautering) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 347

title: Shama'il al-Muhammadiya 348
englishRaw:
Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
                      "Messenger of Allah ﷺ took treatment of cupping on the back of his leg at Milal (a place about seventeen miles-27 km-from Madinah Munawwarah in the direction of Makkah) while he was in the state of ihraam".
narrator: Anas bin Maalik (may Allah be pleased with him) reports:
body: "Messenger of Allah ﷺ took treatment of cupping on the back of his leg at Milal (a place about seventeen miles-27 km-from Madinah Munawwarah in the direction of Makkah) while he was in the state of ihraam".
arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، قَالَ: أَنْبَأَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ ﷺ احْتَجَمَ وَهُوَ مُحْرِمٌ بَمَلَلٍ عَلَى ظَهْرِ الْقَدَمِ.
references: Book of Hajaamah (Cupping-Cautering) Of Prophet of Allah ﷺ, Hadith 348
